metadata.purpose: Domain-level case studies within each framework area, generated via the ictd-case-study-generation skill (v2: stage1 names + stage2 assignments + stage2.5 grounded descriptions + cross-area merge) using the Claude API.
metadata.schema_note: topics_covered/cross_cutting_axes are auto-derived, not independently curated by the model. background_concepts URLs were grounded via web search but should still be spot-checked before publishing.
metadata.coverage_note: Gap is expected to be almost entirely area_agnostic=true readings.
metadata.assignment_note: Incremental area-by-area assignment via assign_readings_to_examples.py (ollama); no new case studies created.

case_studies.name: Remote Sensing Crop & Land Mapping
case_studies.description: This domain covers machine learning approaches for mapping crops, agricultural fields, and land cover from satellite, drone, and street view imagery, as seen in works like 'OpenMapFlow', 'Fields of The World', and 'Semantic Segmentation of Crop Type in Africa'. It spans crop type classification, field boundary segmentation, yield mapping, and specialized detection tasks such as harvest piles, opium poppy cultivation, and irrigation type. Techniques include transformers, LSTMs, attention networks, transfer learning, and data augmentation applied to Landsat and Sentinel data.

background_concepts.why_needed: Several crop-type and land-cover mapping papers frame the task as semantic segmentation but assume the reader knows what pixel-wise labeling and metrics like mIoU mean.

suggested_resources.title: Semantic Segmentation of Remote Sensing Images: Definition, Methods, Datasets and Applications

background_concepts.why_needed: Mapping pipelines take inputs like Sentinel-2 spectral bands and derived indices as given, without explaining what the bands are or their spatial resolutions.

suggested_resources.title: Sentinel-2 Multispectral Imager | NASA Earthdata
Sentinel 2 Bands and Combinations - GIS Geography

background_concepts.why_needed: Feature engineering in crop mapping frequently relies on NDVI and related indices as inputs, treated as common knowledge.

suggested_resources.title: Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) | NASA Earthdata
What is NDVI? - GIS Geography

case_studies.name: Climate-Agriculture Forecasting & Impact Modeling
case_studies.description: This domain examines how climate change and weather affect agricultural systems through forecasting and impact modeling, including works on precipitation forecasting, drought classification, vegetation greenness prediction, and crop yield loss. Readings like 'Robustly modeling the nonlinear impact of climate change on agriculture' and 'Interpretable machine learning approach to understand U.S. prevented planting events' combine econometrics, physics-informed neural networks, and self-supervised learning. It also addresses adaptation tracking, biological threats like the Quelea bird and wheat streak mosaic virus, and neural weather forecasting.

background_concepts.why_needed: Crop-yield-loss forecasting and weather models invoke PINNs, assuming the reader understands how differential-equation residuals become a training loss term.

suggested_resources.title: A Hands-on Introduction to Physics-Informed Neural Networks (nanoHUB)
Physics-Informed Neural Networks tutorial (PyTorch)

background_concepts.why_needed: The paper combining econometrics and ML assumes familiarity with panel-data / nonlinear temperature-response regressions used to estimate climate impacts on yields.

suggested_resources.title: Differences in Differences (UC Davis lecture notes)

background_concepts.why_needed: Forecasting papers reference S2S lead times and drought indices without defining the forecasting horizons or how they relate to agricultural risk.

suggested_resources.title: NDVI and drought conditions - Climate Data Guide (UCAR)

case_studies.name: Digital Agricultural Extension & Farmer Information Systems
case_studies.description: This domain focuses on ICT-based tools for delivering agricultural knowledge and advice to farmers, including participatory video platforms like 'Digital Green' and interactive voice forums like 'Avaaj Otalo'. Readings evaluate the cost-effectiveness of digital discussion tools, causal inference for recommendations, and the challenges of scaling ICT in smallholder agriculture. It also examines when technology fails to fit farmers' actual information-sharing practices, as in rural China.

background_concepts.concept: Causal inference / difference-in-differences for impact evaluation
background_concepts.why_needed: The paper evaluating digital-agriculture recommendations uses causal inference designs to assess adoption effects, assuming knowledge of treatment/control comparisons.

suggested_resources.title: Difference in differences — PyMC example gallery
Causal Inference using Difference-in-Differences (Lecture 1)

case_studies.name: Agricultural Market Structure & Price Information
case_studies.description: This domain studies agricultural markets, price discovery, and information systems that connect farmers to buyers, including 'Mobile Mandi', crowd-sourced market information in Liberia, and AI-based market intelligence for farmer collectives. Readings analyze the structure and dynamics of India's mandis and rural market towns, alongside price forecasting, anomaly detection, and online price measurement. It also covers policy issues like demonetization's impact on agricultural trade and smallholder market exclusion.

background_concepts.why_needed: Papers on Indian rural markets discuss mandis, licensed intermediaries, and APMC regulation as background institutions without explaining how they structure trade.

suggested_resources.title: Agricultural Produce Market Committee (APMC) Act - BYJU's
Understanding the APMC / Mandi - Rang De

case_studies.name: Smallholder Farmer Livelihoods & Agrarian Crisis
case_studies.description: This domain addresses the socioeconomic realities, struggles, and productivity of smallholder farmers, featuring narrative accounts of India's farm crisis such as 'Ramrao' and 'Everybody Loves a Good Drought'. Readings examine farm size and productivity relationships, financial analysis of rice farming in Kenya, and the consequences of Bt cotton in Odisha. It also explores collective social movement strategies among small farmers.

case_studies.name: Groundwater, Soil & Sustainable Farming Practices
case_studies.description: This domain covers sustainable resource management in agriculture, particularly groundwater depletion in India and soil health, including soil organic carbon estimation via graph neural networks and climate-related features. Readings promote regenerative agriculture through tools like 'Soil Carbon Copilots' and residue density segmentation for tillage monitoring. It also applies causal machine learning and domain adaptation to sustainable soil management and characterizing porous media.

background_concepts.concept: GRACE satellite gravimetry for groundwater storage
background_concepts.why_needed: Groundwater depletion papers rely on GRACE terrestrial-water-storage measurements, assuming readers know gravity anomalies can quantify water mass changes.

suggested_resources.title: Groundwater | GRACE Tellus - NASA JPL
Water Storage | GRACE-FO Science - NASA

background_concepts.why_needed: SOC-estimation and regenerative-agriculture papers assume familiarity with what soil organic carbon is, how it is measured, and how management sequesters it.

suggested_resources.title: What is soil organic carbon (SOC)? - FAO Global Soil Partnership
Soil-Based Carbon Sequestration - MIT Climate Portal

case_studies.name: Agricultural Measurement & Statistical Ground-Truthing
case_studies.description: This domain focuses on accurate measurement and ground-truthing of agricultural variables, including land area measurement ('From Guesstimates to GPStimates') and irrigation detection and method mapping via Sentinel-2 data and datasets like 'IrrMap'. Readings address flowering onset detection in sparse-label contexts and monitoring disruption events through irrigation detection. The emphasis is on generating reliable data foundations for agricultural analysis.

background_concepts.concept: Sentinel-2 30m surface reflectance and revisit cadence
background_concepts.why_needed: The irrigation-detection and IrrMap datasets rely on Sentinel-2 imagery, spatial resolution and revisit frequency as the measurement substrate without introducing the mission's sensing characteristics.

suggested_resources.title: USGS EROS Archive - Sentinel-2 | U.S. Geological Survey

case_studies.name: In-field IoT and sensing for precision agriculture
case_studies.description: This domain covers sensor-based and IoT-enabled systems for precision agriculture, including low-cost radar soil moisture sensing and deep learning frameworks like 'SMArtCast' for forecasting soil moisture. Readings address pest and disease management through datasets like 'Paddy Doctor', integrated pest management with causality and explainability, and chatbot-IoT integration for maize disease detection. It also includes grapevine disease prediction using multi-sensor remote sensing and transformer models.

background_concepts.why_needed: Pest-identification and IPM papers presume knowledge of integrated pest management concepts and disease-forecasting from climate variables.

suggested_resources.title: NDVI Explained (crop health monitoring primer)

case_studies.name: Agricultural supply chain intermediaries and procurement mechanisms
case_studies.description: This domain examines the role of intermediaries and procurement mechanisms in agricultural supply chains, including incentive-compatible mechanisms for input procurement through farmer collectives. Readings analyze intermediary transactions in Kenya's agricultural supply chain and the political economy of sugar mills in India. The focus is on how transactions, incentives, and connections shape farmer outcomes.

background_concepts.concept: Impact evaluation / causal identification of policy interventions
background_concepts.why_needed: The political-connections study estimates causal effects of institutional arrangements on supply chains, assuming familiarity with quasi-experimental identification.

suggested_resources.title: Causal Inference using Difference-in-Differences (Lecture 1)

case_studies.name: Human-centered design of low-cost agricultural tools and machines
case_studies.description: This domain focuses on designing affordable, appropriate agricultural tools and machinery for smallholder contexts, including developing intermediate machines for high-land agriculture. Readings apply human-centered design principles, as in redesigning agricultural hand tools in Western Kenya within an ICTD framework. The emphasis is on matching technology to local needs and constraints.

case_studies.name: Climate-smart farming practices and carbon/emissions in agriculture
case_studies.description: This domain examines how advanced computational methods can improve agricultural sustainability, including using deep reinforcement learning and large-scale simulations to optimize soil carbon sequestration and crop management. It also covers the measurement of greenhouse gas emissions through carbon-smart farming practices and participatory sensing, with a specific focus on rice cultivation. Together, these readings connect AI-driven decision-making with the goal of reducing agriculture's carbon footprint.

background_concepts.why_needed: The soil-carbon RL optimization and carbon-smart farming papers assume the reader understands how management practices transfer atmospheric CO2 into soil organic matter and why this is a climate mitigation lever, without teaching the process.

suggested_resources.title: Soil Organic Carbon in Temperate Managed Ecosystems: A Primer (USDA Climate Hubs)
Soil Carbon Sequestration—Fundamentals | Ohioline

background_concepts.concept: Reinforcement learning (agent, state, action, reward, policy)
background_concepts.why_needed: The crop-management and soil-carbon optimization papers frame farm decisions as an RL/MDP problem and assume familiarity with the agent-environment loop and reward maximization.

suggested_resources.title: Part 1: Key Concepts in RL — OpenAI Spinning Up
🤗 Deep Reinforcement Learning Course - Introduction

case_studies.name: Water and irrigation infrastructure for food/water security
case_studies.description: This domain focuses on infrastructure interventions that secure reliable water and energy access for farming communities across diverse geographies. Readings assess the productivity impact of farm ponds in Northern India, explore solar charging kiosks supporting agricultural business in Kenya, and describe rainwater harvesting paired with automated off-grid irrigation in Guatemala. The common thread is building resilient systems that support both food and water security for underserved regions.

case_studies.name: Alternative and diversified livelihood cultivation systems for smallholders
case_studies.description: This domain explores innovative and diversified cultivation approaches designed to enhance the livelihoods of smallholder and subsistence farmers. It includes AI and IoT-enabled hydroponic grow tents in South Africa, ecosystem-based sustainable aquaculture, modular oyster mushroom cultivation in low-resource settings, and integrated agriculture and clean energy solutions in Sadivayal Village. These readings emphasize accessible, sustainable production methods that expand income opportunities in resource-constrained environments.

case_studies.name: Mobility-Data Epidemic Modeling and Surveillance
case_studies.description: This domain examines how human mobility data, particularly from mobile phone call records, can be leveraged to model and track epidemic spread in developing countries, as seen in studies on COVID-19 in Nigeria during flooding and malaria elimination planning. It combines agent-based and spatiotemporal disease models with mobility signals, while also weighing the challenges of accessing such data and the pitfalls of big data analysis exemplified by the Google Flu parable. Additional readings address forecasting techniques like Bayesian structural time series and measuring how epidemic alerts alter movement patterns.

background_concepts.concept: Call Detail Records (CDRs) as mobility proxy
background_concepts.why_needed: Several readings use mobile phone call records to infer population movement but assume the reader knows what CDRs are, how cell-tower pings approximate location, and their sampling/representativeness biases.

suggested_resources.title: Making the Most of Mobility Data (CDRs): A Guide for Policymakers
What are Call Detail Records (CDR data)? — FlowGeek

background_concepts.why_needed: The agent-based and spatiotemporal disease-spread papers build on standard susceptible-exposed-infectious-recovered dynamics as the underlying disease process, without explaining the compartments or transition rates.

suggested_resources.title: Modeling Epidemics: A Primer (SEIR/compartmental models)

background_concepts.why_needed: The Scott nowcasting reading presents state-space/structural time-series forecasting and 'predicting the present' without introducing the underlying state-space decomposition or spike-and-slab regression basics assumed of the reader.

case_studies.name: AI-Driven Vaccination and Immunization Uptake
case_studies.description: This domain focuses on using AI and optimization to increase vaccine uptake, centered on the ADVISER system deployed in Nigeria for child vaccination intervention. It also covers fair vaccination strategies via influence maximization, sequential vaccine allocation under delayed feedback, and Bayesian modeling to correct for maternal recall bias in immunization rate estimates in India.

background_concepts.concept: Influence maximization / seed-set selection on networks
background_concepts.why_needed: The fair-vaccination paper frames vaccine promotion as choosing a 'seed set' to maximize spread over a social/contact network, assuming familiarity with diffusion models (independent cascade / linear threshold) and the greedy submodular approximation.

suggested_resources.title: WSDM 2018 Tutorial on Influence Maximization in Online Social Networks

case_studies.name: Restless Bandits and Resource Allocation for Public Health Outreach
case_studies.description: This domain applies restless bandits and reinforcement learning to allocate scarce health resources fairly and effectively in underserved communities, including index-based treatment allocation and Bayesian online approaches. Readings address maternal and child healthcare in non-Markovian settings, deep reinforcement learning for equitable resource distribution, and planning multiple epidemic interventions.

background_concepts.concept: Restless multi-armed bandits and the Whittle index
background_concepts.why_needed: These papers assume the reader understands RMABs, the passive/active arm formulation, indexability, and the Whittle index policy used to prioritize which beneficiaries to allocate limited health-worker attention to.

suggested_resources.title: Markovian restless bandits and index policies: A review (intro sections)

case_studies.name: AI-Assisted Medical Imaging Diagnosis in Low-Resource Settings
case_studies.description: This domain covers deep learning tools for diagnosing diseases from medical signals and images in low-resource areas, spanning cough-sound-based pulmonary screening, head and neck cancer triage, and pediatric oncology support for marginalized communities. Several readings focus specifically on detecting occupational lung diseases like silicosis and pneumoconiosis from chest X-rays.

background_concepts.concept: Convolutional neural networks for medical image classification
background_concepts.why_needed: The chest X-ray silicosis/pneumoconiosis and cancer-screening papers assume the reader understands CNN building blocks (convolution, pooling, fully-connected layers, AUC evaluation) used to classify medical images.

suggested_resources.title: A practical introduction to convolutional neural networks for medical professionals
Deep learning: CNNs for medical image classification (IMAIOS)

background_concepts.why_needed: SHIELD is described as a 'self-supervised hierarchical imaging framework,' assuming the reader knows how models learn representations from unlabeled images (pretext tasks, contrastive learning), which is essential in label-scarce clinical settings.

suggested_resources.title: Self-Supervised Learning: Self-Prediction and Contrastive Learning (NeurIPS 2021 tutorial slides)

case_studies.name: Maternal, Child, and Reproductive Health Digital Systems
case_studies.description: This domain explores digital tools and design approaches supporting maternal, child, and reproductive health in low-resource settings, including tele-triage for pregnant women in Kenya, self-directed learning for maternity workers, and OCR digitization of mother and child protection cards in India. It also addresses community cohesion among mothers in bandwidth-constrained settings, birth declaration to reduce child marriage in Bangladesh, tangible nutrition play in Peru, and causal analysis of contraceptive discontinuation.

background_concepts.concept: Causal inference from observational data (potential outcomes)
background_concepts.why_needed: The contraceptive-discontinuation paper applies causal inference to survey data, assuming familiarity with the potential-outcomes framework, counterfactuals, and confounding rather than teaching them.

suggested_resources.title: Causal Inference and Potential Outcomes (UCL PUBL0050 lecture notes)
Causal inference and effect estimation using observational data (glossary)

case_studies.name: Climate-Health and Air Pollution Sensing
case_studies.description: This domain integrates climate and air-quality sensing with health outcomes, covering climate-driven epidemic prediction, foundation models for emission sources and respiratory infection hotspots, and transformer-based air pollution source mapping in South Asia. Readings also address mitigating kiln industry pollution, wildfire smoke plume segmentation from satellite imagery, high-resolution road-network pollutant quantification, and extreme weather impacts on physical activity and sleep.

background_concepts.concept: Satellite remote sensing and multispectral imagery
background_concepts.why_needed: The emission-mapping, kiln-classifier, and wildfire-smoke papers rely on multispectral and geostationary satellite bands, spatial/spectral/temporal resolution tradeoffs, and image preprocessing that are assumed rather than explained.

suggested_resources.title: Fundamentals of Remote Sensing (NASA ARSET self-paced course)
Introduction to Spatial and Spectral Resolution: Multispectral Imagery (Earth Lab)

case_studies.name: Clean Cooking and Household Energy Transitions
case_studies.description: This domain addresses transitions to cleaner household cooking energy, using a health belief model and theory of change for service design in the slums of Kathmandu. It also assesses cooking energy behavior and the potential for shifting to electric cooking in informal urban settlements.

background_concepts.why_needed: The 'theory of change for cleaner cooking' paper explicitly builds a Health Belief Model for service design, assuming the reader knows its constructs (perceived susceptibility, severity, benefits, barriers, cues to action, self-efficacy).

suggested_resources.title: The Health Belief Model — Methods for Stress Management (Pressbooks)
Understanding the Health Belief Model — A Primer

case_studies.name: Human-Centered Global Health Device Design
case_studies.description: This domain focuses on designing and prototyping medical devices for global health, including life-saving warming technologies and stakeholder engagement through front-end device design. Readings also cover a usability study of an optical schistosomiasis diagnostic device in Nigeria and efforts to transform rural healthcare through technology crafts for the digitally underserved.

case_studies.name: Designing for Stigmatized and Marginalized Care Recipients
case_studies.description: This domain examines human-centered design for stigmatized and marginalized populations, including Rohingya refugees in Bangladesh, caregivers of children with autism facing stigmatization, and adolescents managing Type 1 diabetes in LMICs. It also considers the paradoxes of India's vast population and untapped human resources within health care.

case_studies.name: Secure and Agile mHealth Deployment in the Field
case_studies.description: This domain addresses the operational, security, and organizational challenges of deploying mobile health systems in the field, including data classification for secure health data collection and security leadership where there is no CISO. It also covers applying agile principles to complex public health problems like malnutrition management and the adoption of mHealth services by internally displaced persons in Nigeria.

background_concepts.concept: CISO role and information security governance / data classification
background_concepts.why_needed: The paper's title and argument revolve around the absence of a Chief Information Security Officer and data-security classification schemes, assuming familiarity with security governance roles and data-classification mechanisms.

suggested_resources.title: DHIS2 Implementation Guide - security and data ownership section

case_studies.name: Health Information System (HIS/DHIS2) Implementation in LMICs
case_studies.description: This domain examines the deployment of open-source health information systems, particularly DHIS2, across low- and middle-income countries including Lao PDR, Mozambique, Lesotho, and Malawi. The readings explore how these systems can strengthen representation and inclusion of marginalized groups, while also documenting the complexity, distress, and social exclusion risks that arise during implementation. Technical themes include integrating community and facility health data through methods like machine learning and probabilistic record linkage.

background_concepts.why_needed: Readings in this domain assume familiarity with DHIS2's architecture, aggregate vs. case-based data, and the HISP implementation ecosystem.

suggested_resources.title: What is DHIS2? — DHIS2 Documentation

case_studies.name: mHealth Behavior Change and Frontline Health Worker Support
case_studies.description: This domain covers the design and sustainability of mobile health interventions aimed at behavior change communication and supporting frontline health workers, with case studies from rural India and sub-Saharan Africa. It addresses both the strategic development of mHealth programs and the training and support practices needed to sustain them. It also includes technical approaches such as metric learning for handwritten digit recognition to expand the reach of voice-based communication platforms.

background_concepts.why_needed: The Uttar Pradesh case study develops a behavior change communication strategy assuming knowledge of the underlying behavior-change theory that structures such interventions.

suggested_resources.title: The Health Belief Model - Rural Health Promotion and Disease Prevention Toolkit

case_studies.name: AI/ML for Clinical Prediction and Health Equity
case_studies.description: This domain applies artificial intelligence and machine learning to clinical prediction tasks such as diabetes forecasting, chronic disease management through lab test response prediction, and suicide risk detection, alongside foundational methods like contrastive learning and self-supervised time-series clustering. A strong equity thread runs throughout, with readings on quantifying health inequalities induced by data and AI, achieving gender fairness in mental health prediction, and mapping vulnerability among disadvantaged urban populations. It also spans related predictive applications including insurance fraud detection and satellite-imagery-based poverty estimation.

background_concepts.why_needed: The subject-wise clustering and online contrastive-learning papers assume the reader understands how contrastive objectives (positive/negative pairs, embedding space) learn representations from largely unlabeled data.

suggested_resources.title: Contrastive Learning: A Tutorial (Built In)

case_studies.name: Digital Technology in Pandemic Response and Risk Communication
case_studies.description: This domain focuses on the social and technological dimensions of the COVID-19 pandemic in South Asia, examining impacts on poor and vulnerable groups in India and gender inequality among healthcare workers in Pakistan. It highlights the role of technology in enabling risk communication and community engagement, drawing on experiences from Bangladesh during the pandemic.

background_concepts.why_needed: The Bangladesh paper builds on the established public-health RCCE framework used during outbreaks, referencing it as a known institutional mechanism without introducing it.

case_studies.name: Gig/platform labour precarity and worker agency
case_studies.description: This domain examines the precarious conditions faced by gig and platform workers, particularly ride-hailing and delivery workers across the Global South, as seen in studies of UberGo drivers, Cape Town ride-hailing, and delivery workers in urban India. It attends to how gendered inequalities shape the gig economy in places like New Delhi, how AI management affects worker pay and motivation, and how workers exercise agency through gamification and risk-mitigation strategies. It also considers pathways for facilitating gig work opportunities, especially for youth in developing countries.

background_concepts.why_needed: These ethnographic studies of ride-hailing and delivery workers assume the reader understands algorithmic management—how platforms delegate task allocation, monitoring, and discipline to algorithms rather than human managers—without defining it.

suggested_resources.title: Gig Work, Algorithmic Technologies, and the Uncertain Future of Work (open chapter)

background_concepts.why_needed: The 'two-fold gamification' study analyzes how delivery-platform incentive design manipulates worker behavior, but assumes familiarity with game-design elements (points, badges, leaderboards, extrinsic vs. intrinsic motivation) as applied to non-game contexts.

suggested_resources.title: What is Gamification? — Interaction Design Foundation
Gamification and Game-Based Learning — University of Waterloo CTE

background_concepts.why_needed: The 'freedom-based inclusion' framing in the gendered-inequality paper draws directly on Amartya Sen's capability approach (functionings, agency, substantive freedoms) without explaining this theoretical foundation.

suggested_resources.title: Sen's Capability Approach — Internet Encyclopedia of Philosophy

case_studies.name: Algorithmic fairness interventions for platform workers
case_studies.description: This domain focuses on designing technical and algorithmic interventions to improve labour conditions for platform workers. The readings address creating dynamic checklists through Bayesian case-based reasoning to promote decent work and developing scheduling mechanisms that guarantee fair wages for food delivery workers.

background_concepts.concept: Algorithmic management / work allocation on platforms
background_concepts.why_needed: These CS system-design papers propose fair-wage scheduling and dynamic safety checklists as interventions layered onto platform work-allocation systems; they assume the reader knows how baseline algorithmic task/wage allocation on gig platforms functions.

suggested_resources.title: The Algorithmic-Human Manager: work allocation and wage determination (open primer sections)

case_studies.name: MGNREGA / public employment guarantee as climate and welfare infrastructure
case_studies.description: This domain explores India's MGNREGA public employment guarantee scheme as a dual mechanism for welfare provision and climate resilience. The readings cover tools like CRISP-M for climate-resilient planning, pro-poor climate support through social protection and small-scale infrastructure, and the carbon sequestration co-benefits achievable through MGNREGS activities. Together they frame public works employment as an infrastructure linking poverty alleviation with environmental mitigation.

background_concepts.why_needed: All these papers presuppose knowledge of MGNREGA's institutional mechanics—100 days of guaranteed wage work, demand-driven design, Gram Panchayat implementation, and the water/soil/afforestation asset works that generate climate co-benefits.

suggested_resources.title: MGNREGS — Drishti IAS government schemes guide
MGNREGA case study — UCLG-CISDP observatory (PDF)

background_concepts.why_needed: The carbon co-benefit papers quantify carbon sequestered by MGNREGA's land/water/afforestation works but assume the reader understands the physical process by which atmospheric CO2 is transferred into soil organic carbon and biomass.

suggested_resources.title: Soil Carbon Sequestration—Fundamentals — Ohio State Ohioline
Soil Carbon Sequestration — FAO Soils Portal

background_concepts.why_needed: These carbon co-benefit studies quantify sequestration from MGNREGA works (water conservation, afforestation) and assume the reader knows the scheme's structure and permissible works.

suggested_resources.title: Vikaspedia: Overview of MGNREGA

case_studies.name: Welfare allocation equity and datafication of marginalized workers
case_studies.description: This domain investigates equity and discrimination in the distribution of social protection benefits, particularly in India's National Rural Employment Guarantee Scheme. The readings analyze caste-based inequities in welfare allocation, the datafication encounters that reproduce discrimination for marginalized workers, and the broader structural challenges of India's social protection system.

background_concepts.concept: Caste system and Scheduled Castes/Scheduled Tribes
background_concepts.why_needed: The caste-inequity analyses assume the reader understands India's caste hierarchy, the constitutional Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe categories, and reservation policy that structure how welfare discrimination is measured.

suggested_resources.title: An introduction to the basic elements of the caste system of India — Frontiers
Scheduled Caste — INSIGHTS IAS explainer

background_concepts.why_needed: Studies of datafied welfare allocation and 'datafication encounters' assume knowledge of India's Aadhaar biometric ID and DBT infrastructure through which benefits are digitally targeted and can encode exclusion of marginalized workers.

suggested_resources.title: Aadhaar for Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) — UIDAI (Government of India)
Direct Benefit Transfer & the JAM Trinity explained

case_studies.name: Digital traces for labour market and migration measurement
case_studies.description: This domain examines how digital data sources can be used to measure labour market dynamics and human mobility. The readings demonstrate using social media to track labour market flows and unemployment, mobile phone call records to infer internal migration in Rwanda, and phone data to study cash transfers and phone sharing in Togo, alongside survey-based analysis of India's labour market changes.

background_concepts.why_needed: The Rwanda migration inference and Togo phone-sharing papers derive mobility and labour proxies from CDRs, assuming the reader knows what these mobile-operator records contain (caller/callee, time, duration, cell-tower location) and how tower data approximates location.

suggested_resources.title: Making the Most of Mobility Data (CDRs): A Guide for Policymakers — MIT GOV/LAB
How Analysis of Call Detail Records Provides Information for Development — ALNAP

case_studies.name: Digital platforms for social protection and inclusive employment access
case_studies.description: This domain explores how digital technologies and platforms can expand access to social benefits and employment for marginalized and disadvantaged populations. The readings cover smartphone usage among emergent users in India, cashless transaction adoption in Bangladesh, technology-enabled benefit access for seasonal migrant workers, and digital pathways to employment for Hijra (third gender) communities. It also includes frameworks like VulnerabilityMap for identifying and mapping vulnerable urban populations.

background_concepts.concept: Hijra / third-gender communities in South Asia
background_concepts.why_needed: The Hijra employment-pathway paper assumes familiarity with hijra as a recognized third-gender community in South Asia and their historically marginalized socio-economic position, which shapes the inclusion intervention.

background_concepts.concept: Direct Benefit Transfer / Aadhaar-linked social protection
background_concepts.why_needed: Papers on connecting marginalized groups to social benefits assume the reader knows India's Aadhaar-linked DBT welfare-delivery infrastructure that civil-society intermediaries help populations navigate.

suggested_resources.title: Aadhaar for Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) — UIDAI (Government of India)

case_studies.name: Migrant and refugee worker safety and ICT-mediated support
case_studies.description: This domain addresses how information and communication technologies support the safety, solidarity, and resilience of migrant and refugee workers in crisis situations. The readings examine ICT use and hope-building among Rohingya refugees in Bangladesh, Indian migrant workers navigating ICTs during the pandemic, and advocacy efforts toward social safety for migrant workers.

case_studies.name: ICT-Enabled Citizen Accountability and Grievance Redressal
case_studies.description: This domain examines how information and communication technologies enable citizens to monitor government spending, access local services, and hold bureaucracies accountable, as seen in initiatives like 'Our Money, Our Accounts' and Karnataka's helpline and Aasthi systems. It includes studies of voice-based citizen journalism platforms like CGNet Swara and broader analyses of ICT use in Indian governance. The readings also critically explore the surveillance dimensions of these technologies, addressing how bureaucrats both use and subvert digital tools.

background_concepts.concept: Social audit / MGNREGA public hearings (jan sunwai)
background_concepts.why_needed: The MKSS 'Hamara Paisa, Hamara Hisab' and helpline/accountability readings assume familiarity with India's social audit movement and the MGNREGA social-audit mechanism as the institutional backdrop for citizen accountability, but do not explain how these processes work.

suggested_resources.title: A Guide to Conducting Social Audits (Accountability India)
Social Audits Overview (Government of Meghalaya State Rural Employment Society)

case_studies.name: Datafication of Informal Urban Settlements
case_studies.description: This domain explores how data collection and datafication processes affect informal urban settlements and slums, particularly in the global south, through frameworks of data justice and case studies like Quarry Road West in Durban. It engages with debates over smart city citizenship, participation, and the power dynamics of big data in public service organizations. The readings collectively interrogate how making marginalized communities 'visible' through data can advance or undermine justice and rights.

background_concepts.why_needed: Several of these readings apply or extend an 'applied data justice framework' and invoke visibility/(dis)engagement/anti-discrimination pillars without teaching the underlying concept of data justice they build on.

suggested_resources.title: What Is Data Justice? (Royal Roads University LibGuide)
Framing data justice: Taylor's three pillars (primer)

case_studies.name: Satellite Imagery for Development Measurement and Targeting
case_studies.description: This domain covers the use of satellite imagery and remote sensing to measure socio-economic indicators, classify land cover, detect urban slums, and track the evolution of cities. It includes methodological work on datasets like IndiaSat and night-time luminosity as proxies for economic activity, as well as applications for targeting villages for rural development. The readings also extend to using imagery to assess regional inequality and the effects of international sanctions, as in North Korea.

background_concepts.concept: Nighttime lights (DMSP-OLS / VIIRS) as an economic proxy
background_concepts.why_needed: These readings use night-time luminosity as a stand-in for economic activity and specific sensors (DMSP-OLS, VIIRS) without explaining how the data is collected or why brightness correlates with GDP.

suggested_resources.title: Illuminating Economic Growth Using Satellite Images (IMF Finance & Development)
Light Every Night – nighttime light data set and tools (World Bank Data Blog)

background_concepts.concept: Supervised land-cover / pixel-level classification of satellite imagery
background_concepts.why_needed: The IndiaSat, urban-slum-detection and city-evolution papers assume knowledge of supervised land-cover classification, spectral bands, and training/ground-truth workflows that they use but don't introduce.

suggested_resources.title: ARSET – Land Cover Classification with Satellite Imagery (NASA)
Introduction to LULC Mapping – Land Use Land Cover Guide

case_studies.name: Political Connections, Patronage, and Rent Capture
case_studies.description: This domain investigates how firms benefit from political ties, focusing on the mechanisms by which connected companies gain preferential access to finance, contracts, and resources. Readings examine quid pro quo arrangements between builders and politicians in election finance, lender favoritism toward connected firms, and rent capture in specific industries such as India's sugar mills.

background_concepts.why_needed: The quantitative rent-capture papers estimate the causal effect of political connections using panel and difference-in-differences style designs and rely on the parallel-trends logic without explaining it.

suggested_resources.title: Difference-in-Differences (World Bank DIME Wiki)
An Introduction to Difference-in-Difference Analysis (Tilburg Science Hub)

case_studies.name: Electoral Cycles in Public Service Provision
case_studies.description: This domain analyzes how electoral timing influences the delivery of public services, with a specific focus on electricity provision and losses in India. Using quasi-experimental evidence from special elections, the readings demonstrate how political incentives shape patterns of service allocation and utility performance around election cycles.

background_concepts.why_needed: Both papers exploit election timing as a natural experiment and rely on quasi-experimental estimators (RDD, DiD) whose identifying assumptions are assumed rather than taught.

suggested_resources.title: Regression Discontinuity Designs in Economics (Lee & Lemieux user guide)
Difference-in-Differences (World Bank DIME Wiki)

background_concepts.why_needed: Min's work measures 'electricity losses' as the political manipulation variable, which requires understanding technical vs. commercial T&D loss as a metric — used without definition.

suggested_resources.title: Light Every Night – measuring energy access with nighttime lights (World Bank)

case_studies.name: Elite Power and Structural Inequality (Foundational Critique)
case_studies.description: This domain provides foundational theoretical critiques of concentrated power and economic inequality, drawing on classic works like Mills' 'The Power Elite' and Domhoff's class-domination theory of power. It includes analyses of rising inequality in wealth and its societal dangers, as articulated by Piketty and Stiglitz, alongside examinations of persistent poverty amid growing affluence in the new India.

case_studies.name: E-Participation and Digital Democracy Platforms
case_studies.description: This domain examines the design and adoption of digital platforms intended to foster citizen engagement and participation in governance across cities like Windhoek, Cape Town, and Mexico. The readings analyze factors affecting citizens' uptake of e-participation tools and how such platforms can address mistrust while strengthening democratic practices.

case_studies.name: Digital Identity, Documentation, and Exclusion
case_studies.description: This domain explores how digital identification and documentation systems, such as India's Aadhaar and birth declaration processes in rural Bangladesh, affect access to rights and services. The readings highlight how these systems can both serve social goals like reducing child marriage and produce exclusions that marginalize vulnerable communities.

background_concepts.why_needed: The MarginalizedAadhaar documentary and related readings assume the reader knows how Aadhaar enrolment, biometric authentication, and its linkage to services work when documenting denial-of-service exclusions.

suggested_resources.title: Aadhaar: A Complete Guide to India's Unique Identification System
ID systems analysed: Aadhaar (Privacy International)

case_studies.name: ICT for Refugee and Stateless Populations
case_studies.description: This domain focuses on how information and communication technologies support Rohingya refugees in Bangladesh, addressing themes of solidarity, leadership, negotiation, and hope. The readings examine refugees' aspirations for democratic participation and self-determination in the digital age amid statelessness.

case_studies.name: Media, Information, and Policy Discourse
case_studies.description: This domain analyzes the role of traditional and digital media in shaping political discourse, polarization, and policy debates. It includes studies of the political blogosphere and Twitter polarization in the US context, alongside analyses of media bias in Indian policy discourse and the media's role in monitoring social welfare schemes.

case_studies.name: Open Data and Aid Transparency
case_studies.description: This domain examines how open data practices are transforming the international aid sector, as reflected in readings like 'How open data is changing international aid' and 'The next web.' It also covers methods for classifying and tracking aid contributions in relation to development goals, drawing on 'Classifying and Tracking International Aid Contribution Towards SDGs.'

case_studies.name: Community Telecenters and Intermediaries
case_studies.description: This domain explores the role of community-based telecenters and multimedia centers in expanding technology access, with particular attention to the pivotal yet complicated role of human intermediaries. Grounded in readings on 'The Complex Position of the Intermediary in Telecenters' and the 'Sustainability Failures of Rural Telecenters' from the SARI project, it interrogates the operational and sustainability challenges these initiatives face in rural settings.

case_studies.name: Digital delivery of social protection entitlements
case_studies.description: This domain investigates how digital systems, including India's Aadhaar identification program, mediate the delivery of social protection benefits and often produce exclusion, as seen in 'MarginalizedAadhaar' and 'Technologies that Disempower.' It examines design flaws, grievance redress, and citizen-assistance mechanisms, while also considering broader economic shocks like the effects of demonetization on domestic agricultural trade.

background_concepts.concept: Public Distribution System (PDS) and Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT)
background_concepts.why_needed: This domain's readings assume how India's entitlement pipelines (PDS rations, DBT subsidies/pensions) authenticate and pay beneficiaries — the institutional mechanism into which authentication is inserted — without explaining it.

suggested_resources.title: Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT): JAM Trinity Explained
What Is Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT)? (Bank of Baroda)

case_studies.name: Technology-assisted grievance redress and citizen engagement in welfare programs
case_studies.description: This domain focuses on technological and civil-society mechanisms for improving transparency, accountability, and citizen participation in social welfare schemes, exemplified by systems like 'Airavat' and technology-assisted grievance redressal models in rural India. It also considers the roles of citizen engagement, mass media monitoring, and systemic reform in strengthening the performance of welfare and education programs.

background_concepts.concept: Grievance redressal mechanism (institutional structure in Indian governance)
background_concepts.why_needed: These readings build feedback and complaint loops on top of India's formal grievance redress architecture (nodal officers, escalation, CPGRAMS-style portals); they assume readers know how a government grievance mechanism is supposed to function.

suggested_resources.title: CPGRAMS — Centralised Public Grievance Redress and Monitoring System (official portal)
Grievance Redress Mechanism in Government (DFE/Government of India guide, PDF)

background_concepts.concept: Social audit as a social-accountability mechanism
background_concepts.why_needed: Civil-society-mediated redress and transparency systems for welfare schemes draw on the social-audit tradition (Gram Sabha verification of records against ground reality under MGNREGA Section 17); the readings assume this participatory-accountability model.

suggested_resources.title: Social Audits in MGNREGS (Drishti IAS)
A Guide to Conducting Social Audits (Accountability India, PDF)

background_concepts.concept: Welfare scheme entitlements (PDS/MGNREGA/pensions) that grievances concern
background_concepts.why_needed: Building engagement into 'welfare schemes' presumes the reader knows the specific entitlement programs (rations under NFSA, employment under MGNREGA) whose exclusion errors generate the complaints being surfaced.

suggested_resources.title: National Food Security Act, 2013 (BYJU'S IAS notes)

case_studies.name: Satellite imagery and geospatial ML for governance and monitoring
case_studies.description: This domain applies satellite imagery and geospatial machine learning to governance, humanitarian, and monitoring challenges, including predicting socio-economic indicators, tracking urban growth in flood-risk areas, and detecting small vessels or brick kilns. Through tools like 'SatDash,' 'VulnerabilityMap,' and 'Space to Policy,' it demonstrates how planetary-scale geospatial data can support policy compliance, disaster assessment, and mapping of vulnerable populations.

background_concepts.concept: Supervised classification / semantic segmentation of satellite imagery
background_concepts.why_needed: The urban-growth monitoring, land-damage, and vessel-detection systems apply pixel-level classification and segmentation of remote-sensing imagery as a core method that is assumed rather than introduced.

suggested_resources.title: ARSET – Land Cover Classification with Satellite Imagery (NASA)
Tutorial: Remote Sensing Image Classification with QGIS (OCWGIS)

case_studies.name: Satellite & Mobile-Data Poverty Mapping
case_studies.description: This domain covers computational methods for estimating poverty and wealth from non-traditional data sources, particularly satellite imagery and mobile phone records, as seen in readings like "Combining satellite imagery and machine learning to predict poverty" and "Predicting Poverty and Wealth from Mobile Phone Data." It addresses technical approaches such as transfer learning, object detection, and interpretable mapping, alongside applied cases like targeting villages for rural development and cash transfers in Togo. It also engages critical concerns including fairness, urban-rural disparities, and how often prediction models must be updated to remain accurate.

background_concepts.why_needed: The Blumenstock CDR poverty work and the Togo phone-sharing study treat mobile-operator call/SMS logs and tower locations as the raw data source without explaining what fields a CDR contains or how location is inferred from cell towers.

suggested_resources.title: Mobile Phone Network Data for Development: How Analysis of Call Detail Records (CDRs) Provide Valuable Information (ALNAP)
Call detail record (Wikipedia overview)

background_concepts.concept: Transfer learning with CNNs / feature extraction
background_concepts.why_needed: Xie and Jean build poverty predictors by taking ImageNet-pretrained convolutional networks and repurposing them for satellite imagery, assuming the reader understands pretraining, fine-tuning, and using a CNN as a fixed feature extractor.

suggested_resources.title: CS231n — Transfer Learning (Stanford lecture notes)
A Gentle Introduction to Transfer Learning for Deep Learning

background_concepts.concept: Nighttime lights as an economic proxy
background_concepts.why_needed: Jean's method uses satellite nighttime luminosity as a training signal for daytime imagery, presuming familiarity with how brightness pixels correlate with economic activity and their known saturation/rural limitations.

suggested_resources.title: Illuminating Economic Growth Using Satellite Images (IMF Finance & Development)

background_concepts.why_needed: The fairness-in-satellite-poverty-maps paper and the interpretable-maps work assume knowledge of fairness harms, group disparities, and how bias enters an ML pipeline (e.g., urban-rural error imbalance).

suggested_resources.title: An Introduction to Algorithmic Fairness (lecture notes)

case_studies.name: Mobile Money & Digital Financial Inclusion
case_studies.description: This domain examines the adoption, impact, and limitations of mobile money and digital financial services in low- and middle-income countries, drawing on randomized control trials in Afghanistan and systematic reviews of financial services in poorer economies. Readings explore risk sharing after natural disasters, state-led inclusion models, and how underserved microentrepreneurs domesticate mobile payments. It also considers the broader roles of mobile money agents as general ICT intermediaries beyond finance, as in the Tanzania study.

background_concepts.why_needed: The Afghanistan mobile-money study and the systematic reviews of mobile financial services report treatment/control effects and average treatment effects without explaining randomized-evaluation design, which underpins their causal claims.

suggested_resources.title: A Beginner's Guide to Randomized Evaluations in Development Economics
An Interactive Introduction to Randomized Control Trials (SERC/Carleton)

background_concepts.concept: Risk sharing / informal insurance after shocks
background_concepts.why_needed: The risk-sharing-and-mobile-phones paper hinges on the economics concept that households smooth consumption via transfer networks after disasters, a mechanism the paper uses but does not teach.

suggested_resources.title: Illuminating Economic Growth Using Satellite Images (IMF F&D) — for context on shock measurement

case_studies.name: Microfinance & Alternative Credit Provision
case_studies.description: This domain traces microfinance from its origins, including Muhammad Yunus's founding vision, through contemporary practice as documented in the Bharat Microfinance Report 2016 and studies of borrower creditworthiness in India's urban slums. It examines lending mechanisms, credit scoring alternatives, and the influence of politics on credit access through works like "Do Lenders Favor Politically Connected Firms?" It also probes marketing and consumer credit dynamics via field experiments on advertising content.

background_concepts.why_needed: The Bharat Microfinance Report, the creditworthiness case study, and the Grameen video assume the reader knows how group/solidarity lending and joint liability substitute for collateral to enable lending to the poor.

suggested_resources.title: Solidarity lending (overview of joint-liability microfinance)

background_concepts.why_needed: The scoping review of alternative credit scoring assumes familiarity with using non-traditional data and ML classifiers to assess creditworthiness and the fairness concerns this raises.

suggested_resources.title: An Introduction to Algorithmic Fairness
Algorithms for Fair Machine Learning: An Introduction

case_studies.name: Mobile Phones for Livelihoods & Market Information
case_studies.description: This domain investigates how mobile phones affect economic activity and market access, anchored by the influential study of India's fishing industry and its critical rebuttal emphasizing complex social context. It explores how diverse users—women livestock keepers in Zimbabwe, Nigerian micro business owners, and emergent smartphone users in India—actually adopt and use mobile technology for their livelihoods. Attention is given to gender gaps and the practical limitations users face despite the sophistication of available tools.

background_concepts.concept: Market information and the law of one price
background_concepts.why_needed: The Kerala fishing study and its critique argue about how phone access reduces price dispersion across markets, assuming the reader grasps arbitrage and price-convergence logic that the papers debate rather than define.

suggested_resources.title: Mobile phones and economic development context — Illuminating Economic Growth (IMF F&D)

case_studies.name: Digital Tools for Women's Economic Empowerment
case_studies.description: This domain focuses on designing and deploying digital and appropriate technologies to economically empower women, especially in rural India, through case studies of Indian women micro-entrepreneurs and smartphone use for rural women. Readings cover user-centered design of tools like a seasonal calendar app and skills-based interventions such as coastal seaweed farming training in Tamil Nadu. It also addresses measuring digital literacy in low-experience populations and appropriate innovations like the pedal-powered washing machine.

background_concepts.why_needed: The seasonal-calendar-app and empowerment studies rely on user-centered design cycles (needs assessment, prototyping, iteration) as their methodological backbone without introducing the practice to non-HCI readers.

suggested_resources.title: Fairness & Algorithmic Decision Making — Introduction (for the analysis-of-pipeline framing)

case_studies.name: Climate Resilience & Community Natural Resource Management
case_studies.description: This domain addresses community-based approaches to climate adaptation and natural resource management, featuring participatory tools like the "CoDriVE-Programme Designer" handbook and the Commons Connect application for vulnerability evaluation. It incorporates landscape ecology concepts, retrospective resilience assessments of semi-arid Indian farming systems, and community consultation methods. The domain also connects poverty and inequality to local natural resource dynamics, including the local natural resource curse.

background_concepts.concept: Climate vulnerability = exposure + sensitivity + adaptive capacity
background_concepts.why_needed: The CoDriVE vulnerability tool and the semi-arid resilience assessment build indices on the IPCC exposure/sensitivity/adaptive-capacity framework, referencing these components as established terminology.

suggested_resources.title: Vulnerability Assessment (USDA Climate Hubs)
Climate Vulnerability and Risk Assessment: Framework, Methods and Guidelines (IISc/IHCAP)

background_concepts.why_needed: The landscape-ecological-concepts planning paper applies patch/matrix/connectivity metrics and fragmentation indices as if the reader already knows this quantitative landscape-ecology vocabulary.

suggested_resources.title: Applying landscape ecological concepts — Exposure/Sensitivity framing via Climate Change Academy methods guide

case_studies.name: Smallholder Agriculture & Rural Innovation Livelihoods
case_studies.description: This domain explores grassroots innovation, entrepreneurship, and livelihood enhancement among smallholder farmers and rural communities, drawing on stories of India's hidden inventors, barefoot movements, and rescuing dancing bears. It examines farm size and productivity debates, effectuation theory in rural entrepreneurship, and integrated agriculture-clean energy solutions in villages like Sadivayal. It also considers how small farmers organize through social movement strategies to build collective agency.

background_concepts.why_needed: The rural-innovation-and-entrepreneurship paper analyzes founders through the lens of effectuation (means-driven, affordable-loss decision logic) without introducing this named theory.

suggested_resources.title: Fairness & Algorithmic Decision Making — Introduction (general reasoning-framework primer)

background_concepts.why_needed: Chand's paper is organized around the long-standing 'inverse relationship' between farm size and per-hectare productivity, a debated agricultural-economics regularity it references as background.

suggested_resources.title: A Beginner's Guide to Randomized Evaluations in Development Economics (for the impact-measurement grounding)

case_studies.name: Data & Analytics for Aid and SDG Tracking
case_studies.description: This domain covers the use of open data, analytics, and classification methods for monitoring international aid, social protection, and progress toward the Sustainable Development Goals. Readings include tools for tracking aid contributions to SDGs, norm-based approaches tackling poverty through anti-discrimination, and analyses of COVID-19's impact on vulnerable groups in India. It also features frameworks for mapping vulnerability among disadvantaged populations and reasoning over longitudinal data to estimate livelihoods in lagging rural regions.

background_concepts.why_needed: The aid-tracking and norm-optimization papers classify flows and progress against Sustainable Development Goal targets and indicators, assuming knowledge of the 17-goal, ~230-indicator UN framework.

suggested_resources.title: SDG Indicators (UN Statistics Division)
E-Handbook on Sustainable Development Goals Indicators (UNESCAP)

case_studies.name: Satellite Imagery and Remote Sensing for Poverty and Socioeconomic Measurement
case_studies.description: This domain applies remote sensing and satellite imagery to measure socioeconomic conditions and economic activity, including temporal prediction of socio-economic indicators and testing whether medium-resolution Landsat imagery can capture activity at small geographies in Vietnam. It draws on near real-time global land use and land cover mapping tools like Dynamic World. It also extends to related forecasting applications such as ultra-short-term photovoltaic power prediction using multi-layer cloud coverage data.

background_concepts.why_needed: The Dynamic World paper produces near-real-time 10 m land-cover class maps, and Goldblatt uses medium-resolution imagery to detect built-up area, both assuming familiarity with per-pixel land-cover classification and spatial resolution.

suggested_resources.title: ML | Transfer Learning with Convolutional Neural Networks (image classification foundations)

background_concepts.concept: Nighttime lights / medium-resolution imagery as economic proxy
background_concepts.why_needed: Goldblatt asks whether medium-resolution imagery can measure economic activity at small geographies, building on the established use of remotely-sensed signals (including night lights) as GDP proxies.

suggested_resources.title: Illuminating Economic Growth Using Satellite Images (IMF F&D)
Measuring quarterly economic growth from outer space (World Bank Blogs)

case_studies.name: Smartphones and mobile learning for teachers and rural learners
case_studies.description: This domain examines how smartphones are adopted and used by emergent and rural users in the Global South, including studies of usage patterns in India and the untapped potential of mobile learning in Ghana's secondary education. It explores the feasibility of introducing mobile technology into schools, such as Kenya's Competency Based Curriculum, and the challenges of integrating smartphones into teacher support programs.

background_concepts.why_needed: The Kenyan feasibility study frames its mobile intervention around schools that 'utilize the Competency Based Curriculum,' assuming the reader knows what CBC replaced (the 8-4-4 system) and how its skills-focused, continuous-assessment design differs from content-based schooling.

suggested_resources.title: Demystifying Competency-Based Curriculum (CBC) – The Kenya Experience
7 Core Competencies of Kenya Competency-Based Curriculum

case_studies.name: AI/ML mapping of educational access and vulnerability
case_studies.description: This domain applies machine learning and satellite imagery to map educational access and socio-economic conditions, including large-scale school mapping through weakly supervised deep learning and temporal prediction of socio-economic indicators. It addresses identifying education deserts and 'last mile' school access in developing countries, as well as broader frameworks for mapping vulnerability among disadvantaged urban populations.

background_concepts.why_needed: The school-mapping paper's title and method hinge on 'weakly supervised' learning to train models without full pixel-level labels, but never explains what weak supervision is versus fully supervised learning.

suggested_resources.title: Weakly Supervised Learning | Baeldung on Computer Science
ECCV 2020 Tutorial on Weakly-Supervised Learning in Computer Vision

background_concepts.concept: Satellite imagery poverty/socio-economic mapping (nighttime lights, transfer learning proxies)
background_concepts.why_needed: These papers infer education deserts and socio-economic indicators from satellite imagery using proxies like nighttime light intensity and transfer learning, but assume familiarity with why remote-sensing proxies stand in for ground survey data.

suggested_resources.title: Using Machine Learning on Satellite Images to Map Poverty | Development Asia
Using Machine Learning to Map Poverty from Satellite Imagery | Geography Realm

case_studies.name: AI tutoring and child-computer interaction for learning
case_studies.description: This domain covers intelligent tutoring systems like AutoTutor and the use of generative AI to support marginalized and rural learners, including applications for equity between rural and urban areas in Brazil. It also examines child-computer interaction in the Global South and specialized AI tools such as sign language interpretation for Nigerian children.

background_concepts.concept: Intelligent Tutoring System (ITS) architecture (student/learner model, expert model, tutor model)
background_concepts.why_needed: The AutoTutor demos and related AI-in-education papers presume the ITS paradigm—that a system tracks a learner model and adapts feedback—without introducing the components (student model, expert model, pedagogical/tutor model) that distinguish ITS from ordinary courseware.

suggested_resources.title: Guiding (digital) learning paths: Intelligent tutoring systems (Lamarr Institute)
Intelligent Tutoring Systems | EBSCO Research Starters

case_studies.name: Digital and data literacy measurement for low-experience populations
case_studies.description: This domain focuses on developing and validating measures of digital literacy specifically for populations with limited internet experience. It also addresses conceptualizing data literacy as an essential competency for participating in an increasingly data-driven society.

background_concepts.concept: Measurement validation / psychometric validity for survey instruments
background_concepts.why_needed: The 'validated digital literacy measures' paper assumes the reader understands what makes a measurement instrument 'validated' (reliability, construct validity), which underpins the whole contribution but is treated as background.

suggested_resources.title: Reliability and Validity of Measurement (Research Methods in Psychology, open text)

case_studies.name: One-device-per-learner hardware initiatives
case_studies.description: This domain covers initiatives that provide individual computing devices to learners, centered on the One Laptop Per Child (OLPC) mission's principles of child empowerment and the XO laptop's design for learning. It also considers sustainable approaches to hardware access, such as reusing landfill-bound computers to bridge the digital divide.

background_concepts.why_needed: The OLPC mission materials are built on Papert's constructionism—learning through building shareable artifacts—and invoke 'child empowerment' and 'design for learning' as if the underlying pedagogical theory is already known to the reader.

suggested_resources.title: Defining constructionist learning (pi-top)
Seymour Papert and Constructionism | EBSCO Research Starters

case_studies.name: Teacher-centered technology support and collaboration
case_studies.description: This domain examines how teachers collaborate and are supported in technology use, including insights into teacher collaboration in West Kenya and design-based research bridging teacher aspirations and goal-setting. It also incorporates systematic review evidence on the factors affecting ICT use in classrooms by teachers.

background_concepts.why_needed: The teacher-aspirations paper announces a 'Design-Based Research approach' as its methodology but assumes readers know DBR's iterative, intervention-in-context cycle rather than treating it as a standard experiment.

suggested_resources.title: Design-Based Research (EdTechBooks – Foundations of Learning and Instructional Design Technology)

background_concepts.why_needed: The ITID reading is explicitly a 'Systematic Review' and assumes familiarity with the protocol-driven search/screening/synthesis process that distinguishes it from a narrative literature review.

suggested_resources.title: Systematic Reviews (University of Maryland Libraries research guide)

case_studies.name: Women's empowerment through educational and vocational technology
case_studies.description: This domain explores technologies designed to empower rural and village women in developing countries, including participatory video and audio platforms using DVDs and IVR systems and smartphone-based empowerment. It features context-specific applications such as vocational education for seaweed farmers in Tamil Nadu and a user-centered seasonal calendar app for women in India.

background_concepts.why_needed: The participatory video/audio platform relies on 'IVR systems' (and DVDs) as its delivery channel for low-literacy communities, but assumes the reader already knows how phone-based menu/voice-prompt systems work and why they suit low-connectivity populations.

suggested_resources.title: Understanding IVR: A Comprehensive Guide to Interactive Voice Response Systems

case_studies.name: System-scale education reform and equity in the Global South
case_studies.description: This domain addresses large-scale education reform and equity efforts, including Teaching at the Right Level programs, barefoot movements, and bold plans to reach millions of out-of-school girls in India. It also engages methods for ensuring fairness and stability in access to education and strategies for fixing broken education systems without additional funding.

background_concepts.why_needed: The GHTC TaRL paper (and the reform TED talks) assume knowledge of Pratham's TaRL model—assessing and grouping children by learning level rather than grade—as the intervention being technologically augmented, without explaining the approach.

suggested_resources.title: Teaching at the Right Level to accelerate learning (J-PAL)
Teaching at the right level (TaRL) | UNICEF FLN Hub

background_concepts.why_needed: The FAiRDAS paper presumes the reader is fluent in algorithmic-fairness terminology and the idea of enforcing fairness constraints over time ('fairness stability'), which it applies to disentangling social inequality in education access.

suggested_resources.title: Fairness (Google Machine Learning Crash Course)

case_studies.name: ICT integration in resource-constrained schools
case_studies.description: This domain examines the integration of ICT in resource-limited educational settings, including mobile cloud remote learning platforms, digital content platforms for sustainable education and disaster resilience, and the constraining and enabling factors of ICT use in rural Nepali schools. It also covers digital technology for sustainable development and cybersecurity concerns in resource-constrained South African schools.

background_concepts.why_needed: The GHTC search-engine paper builds an 'ontology driven search engine' for a mobile cloud remote-learning platform, assuming the reader knows what an ontology is in the semantic-web sense (formal concept/relationship model enabling machine inference) versus a keyword index.

suggested_resources.title: Introduction to the Semantic Web (GraphDB documentation)
An Introduction to the OWL Web Ontology Language (Jeff Heflin, Lehigh)

case_studies.name: Groundwater Depletion Assessment and Sustainable Management in Semi-Arid India
case_studies.description: This domain examines the causes, measurement, and management of groundwater depletion across India, drawing on evidence from Granger causality analyses, GRACE-style storage estimates in the Ganga Basin, and studies linking warming temperatures and cropping intensity to aquifer decline. It also covers assessment tools like satellite-based irrigation detection, hydro-economic optimization of crop patterns, and characterization of the subcontinent's groundwater systems and deeper aquifers. Finally, it addresses sustainable governance and community-based solutions, including system dynamics models for village-level water use and farm-pond intensification.

background_concepts.concept: Specific yield / storage coefficient (converting water-table change to storage volume)
background_concepts.why_needed: Groundwater storage-loss estimation papers multiply water-level declines by specific yield without defining this aquifer property; readers need it to understand how depletion volumes are computed.

suggested_resources.title: Unconfined or water table aquifers (specific yield explained)
How to Calculate the Specific Yield of an Aquifer (worked examples)

background_concepts.concept: GRACE satellite gravimetry for groundwater storage anomalies
background_concepts.why_needed: The Ganga Basin storage-loss paper relies on GRACE-derived terrestrial water storage anomalies as one 'line of evidence' but does not explain how gravity measurements yield groundwater estimates.

suggested_resources.title: Water Storage – NASA GRACE-FO Science

background_concepts.why_needed: The groundwater causality readings apply Granger causality to hydrological time series without introducing the statistical concept of predictive causation.

suggested_resources.title: Diffusion / statistics learning resources — Granger causality note (see time-series causality primers)

case_studies.name: Rainwater Harvesting and Traditional Tank/Watershed Recharge Systems
case_studies.description: This domain focuses on capturing and recharging water through both engineered and traditional structures, including watershed-based tank systems, percolation tanks, and ancient tank cascade systems in semi-arid Southern India. The readings assess the effectiveness and sustainability of these recharge interventions and identify suitable zones for rainwater harvesting to support agricultural intensification in the semi-arid tropics.

background_concepts.concept: Percolation tanks and managed aquifer recharge
background_concepts.why_needed: Readings critique percolation tanks as recharge structures assuming the reader understands how surface impoundments recharge aquifers via infiltration; the recharge mechanism and specific yield govern effectiveness.

suggested_resources.title: Specific Yield & aquifer recharge relationship

case_studies.name: Satellite Remote Sensing and ML for Water/Land Feature Mapping
case_studies.description: This domain covers machine learning methods applied to satellite imagery for mapping water, land, and infrastructure features, spanning object detection (ship detection in SAR, wastewater-treatment monitoring), segmentation, and irrigation-type mapping. It includes tooling and techniques such as OpenMapFlow, data augmentation, attention-based scattering networks, and knowledge-informed deep learning, alongside emerging capabilities like geospatial chain-of-thought reasoning and frame prediction for geostationary imagery.

background_concepts.why_needed: These pipelines assume familiarity with pixel-wise labeling and encoder-decoder segmentation architectures used to extract water and infrastructure features.

suggested_resources.title: U-Net Image Segmentation (PyImageSearch tutorial)

case_studies.name: Precipitation, Weather, and Climate Forecasting via Deep Learning
case_studies.description: This domain encompasses deep learning approaches for weather and climate prediction, including precipitation nowcasting, subseasonal and long-range forecasting, temperature prediction, and extreme weather detection using RNNs, graph neural networks, diffusion models, and autoregressive architectures. It also covers benchmark datasets (WeatherBench, RainBench, ClimateNet), climate data generation, uncertainty handling, and self-supervised representation learning for high-dimensional weather and climate data.

background_concepts.why_needed: Nowcasting readings assume knowledge of radar reflectivity (dBZ) as a proxy for rain rate and the extrapolation framing of short-term forecasting.

suggested_resources.title: Precipitation Nowcasting with Machine Learning (guide)

background_concepts.why_needed: PrecipDiff applies image diffusion models to enhance precipitation observations, assuming the forward/reverse denoising process is understood.

suggested_resources.title: A Friendly Introduction to Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models

case_studies.name: Flood and Drought Risk Modeling and Impact Estimation
case_studies.description: This domain addresses the modeling of flood and drought risks and their societal impacts, combining hydrological AI models, graph neural network flood prediction, and multimodal machine learning approaches. It emphasizes human and infrastructure dimensions, including damage assessment, mobility patterns, socially informed drought estimation, and flood exposure in vulnerable regions like Bangladesh, as well as decision-support and monitoring systems for flood response.

background_concepts.why_needed: The Bangladesh flood-exposure study uses satellite-derived submersion measures whose all-weather radar basis is assumed rather than explained.

suggested_resources.title: Flood mapping with Synthetic Aperture Radar (EO4GEO course)

case_studies.name: Climate Resilience Tools and Participatory Vulnerability Assessment for Rural Livelihoods
case_studies.description: This domain focuses on participatory tools and frameworks for assessing climate vulnerability and building resilience in rural communities, including CoDriVE, Commons Connect, and the CRISP-M tool for MGNREGA planning. It covers community consultation on natural resource management, retrospective resilience assessment of semi-arid farming systems, and field testing of digital tools for water security, linking pro-poor social protection policies to climate adaptation.

background_concepts.concept: MGNREGA (rural employment guarantee and its planning process)
background_concepts.why_needed: CRISP-M and pro-poor climate readings integrate climate info into MGNREGA planning, assuming readers know this demand-driven, Gram-Panchayat-based social protection scheme.

suggested_resources.title: Explained: MGNREGA (rights-based rural employment scheme)
India: MGNREGS (Global Alliance against Hunger and Poverty)

case_studies.name: Nature-Based Solutions and Landscape/Watershed Ecological Planning
case_studies.description: This domain explores ecological and systems-based approaches to landscape and watershed planning, including watershed integrity assessment, landscape ecology metrics, and system dynamics modeling of irrigation efficiency and hydrologic-economic effects. It also examines mental models for water sharing in micro-irrigation, ecological economics modeling, and the broader value and limits of nature-based solutions for addressing climate change and degraded Indian landscapes.

case_studies.name: AI Flood Mapping and Forecasting from Satellite Data
case_studies.description: This domain applies AI to detect and map floods from satellite imagery, leveraging Sentinel-1 SAR and Sentinel-2 optical data, foundation models, semi-supervised learning, and diffusion-based SAR-to-EO translation. It includes large-scale surface water datasets, flood prediction in sparse-observation settings like Kenya, crowdsourcing-enhanced maps, and analyses of human impacts such as mobility and destruction of populated sites during flooding in Nigeria.
