background_concepts.concept: SAR imaging and speckle for flood extent
background_concepts.why_needed: Flood extent mapping readings rely on radar backscatter and elevation-guided cues without explaining SAR imaging principles or speckle noise.

suggested_resources.title: Flood mapping with Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) — FSU Jena course

case_studies.name: Streamflow and Hydrological Modeling with ML
case_studies.description: This domain covers machine learning methods for predicting streamflow and runoff, including LSTMs, transformers, graph neural networks, and vision transformers, often applied in data-sparse or data-scarce regions such as Benin and the Ansongo-Niamey Basin. It emphasizes techniques like domain adaptation, fine-tuning global models with local data, missing-data imputation, and theory-guided or physics-informed learning for flood, drought, and flash flood forecasting.

background_concepts.why_needed: Streamflow-prediction readings assume understanding of the hydrologic cycle, catchments, and how precipitation becomes discharge.

suggested_resources.title: USGS Precipitation-Runoff Modeling System (PRMS)
Rainfall-Runoff Modeling overview (ScienceDirect Topics)

case_studies.name: Remote Sensing Foundation Models and Segmentation Methods
case_studies.description: This domain focuses on foundation models and advanced segmentation techniques for remote sensing, including lightweight pretrained transformers for timeseries, visual prompt-tuning, and adaptations of the Segment Anything Model for change detection. It also covers few-shot instance segmentation, unsupervised change detection for disaster management, geographic location encoders, and machine learning toolkits for Earth system data cubes and coastline delineation.

background_concepts.concept: Foundation models / pretraining and transfer learning
background_concepts.why_needed: These readings assume familiarity with the pretrain-then-finetune paradigm and why it helps data-scarce EO tasks.

suggested_resources.title: Introduction to Earth Foundation Models (IGARSS 2025 tutorial)
Introducing Geospatial Foundation Models in ArcGIS (Esri)

background_concepts.concept: Semantic segmentation and the U-Net architecture
background_concepts.why_needed: Segmentation and change-detection readings assume encoder-decoder segmentation networks and pixel-level labeling are understood.

suggested_resources.title: Satellite imagery segmentation using U-Net (tutorial)

case_studies.name: Tropical Cyclone and Extreme Storm Prediction
case_studies.description: This domain focuses on applying artificial intelligence and generative models to forecast tropical cyclones and their associated hazards. Readings cover techniques such as conditional denoising diffusion models for estimating atmospheric variables from typhoon satellite images, Pix2Pix GANs for wind field diagnosis, and video diffusion models for improved cyclone forecasting. It also addresses 3D cloud reconstruction and enhancing the spatial accuracy of extreme cyclone rainfall in reanalysis data using deep learning.

background_concepts.why_needed: The Pix2Pix cyclone wind-field paper assumes understanding of paired image-to-image translation with GANs, closely related to U-Net generators.

suggested_resources.title: U-Net Image Segmentation (encoder-decoder, Pix2Pix generator basis)

case_studies.name: Coastal Change and Shoreline Forecasting
case_studies.description: This domain examines deep learning approaches for predicting coastal flooding and long-term shoreline evolution under climate change. Readings include climate adaptation-aware and vision-based frameworks for coastal flood prediction that account for shoreline adaptations, as well as forecasting global coastline evolution directly from satellite imagery.

background_concepts.why_needed: Coastline-forecasting readings assume knowledge of how shorelines are extracted from optical imagery (water/land segmentation).

suggested_resources.title: Satellite image segmentation using U-Net for shoreline delineation (overview)

case_studies.name: Climate Downscaling and Simulation Correction
case_studies.description: This domain addresses machine learning methods for correcting biases, downscaling, and emulating climate and Earth system simulations. Readings cover adaptive bias correction for subseasonal forecasting, contrastive learning and image-to-image translation for debiasing and super-resolution, and diffusion-based emulation and downscaling for extreme-event simulation. Additional topics include neural representations for ocean forecasting, radiative transfer modeling, ice flow prediction, and reconstructing land surface boundary conditions.

background_concepts.concept: Statistical vs. dynamical downscaling and GCMs
background_concepts.why_needed: Downscaling readings assume understanding of coarse-resolution GCM outputs and the predictor-predictand statistical relationship.

suggested_resources.title: Climate Model Downscaling (NOAA GFDL)
Statistical downscaling key term (Fiveable Intro to Climate Science)

case_studies.name: Compound Climate Extremes and Disaster Hazard Prediction
case_studies.description: This domain covers the detection and prediction of concurrent and interacting climate hazards such as wildfires, droughts, and heatwaves using spatiotemporal deep learning and remote sensing. Readings include burned area mapping and short-term fire hazard prediction, identifying atmospheric drivers of drought and heat, and predicting the concurrence of heatwaves, droughts, and wildfires. It also spans agent-based climate risk modeling, vision transformers for early climate signal detection, and lightweight networks for extreme weather detection.

background_concepts.why_needed: The concurrence reading assumes the notion of co-occurring hazards (heatwave-drought-wildfire) as an established climate-science concept.

suggested_resources.title: GFDL climate modeling / extremes overview

case_studies.name: AI for Climate Policy and Ecosystem Valuation
case_studies.description: This domain explores AI applications for informing climate policy, valuing ecosystems, and supporting environmental and climate justice. Readings include causal frameworks for ecosystem valuation such as wetland flood mitigation benefits, emulating integrated assessment models, and making climate AI systems past- and future-aware for policy evaluation. It further covers carbon footprint perception, real-time emission management in Kenya, environmental justice policy in Atlanta, climate knowledge bases, and explainable geospatial predictions for climate finance.

background_concepts.concept: Integrated Assessment Models (IAMs) and Social Cost of Carbon
background_concepts.why_needed: ML-IAM and climate-policy readings assume the reader knows what IAMs are and how they couple climate and economic systems to compute policy costs.

suggested_resources.title: Introduction to integrated assessment modeling of climate change (ScienceDirect chapter overview)
Inside the Integrated Assessment Models: Four Issues in Climate Economics (SEI)

case_studies.name: Drought Forecasting and Impact Monitoring
case_studies.description: This domain focuses on forecasting drought severity and duration and monitoring its impacts using deep learning across diverse climate zones. Readings include dedicated datasets like DroughtSet and DroughtED, hybrid recurrent networks, specialized loss functions such as SQUASH, and interpretable methods for extreme-event detection. It also emphasizes localized and socially grounded applications, including Indigenous knowledge-aware monitoring, support for pastoral communities in Northern Kenya, and Twitter-based impact recognition.

background_concepts.why_needed: The S2S extremes reading assumes familiarity with the forecast horizon between weather and climate timescales.

suggested_resources.title: Fiveable Intro to Climate Science (downscaling/prediction terms)

case_studies.name: Weather and Climate Model Emulation and Downscaling
case_studies.description: This domain covers machine learning models that emulate, calibrate, and downscale weather and climate simulations. Readings include benchmark datasets for meteorological downscaling, foundation models such as ClimaX, and emulation of local-scale UK climate models. It also addresses uncertainty quantification and calibration in long-range forecasting with large neural weather models.

background_concepts.concept: Bias correction and super-resolution of meteorological fields
background_concepts.why_needed: The downscaling benchmark assumes understanding of coarse-to-fine mapping and bias correction of model outputs.

suggested_resources.title: Climate Model Downscaling (EPRI Climate Data User Guide)

case_studies.name: Extreme Weather Event Detection and Forecasting
case_studies.description: This domain addresses identifying and forecasting extreme weather and hydrological events under a changing climate. Readings include Bayesian inference of severe hail in Australia, lightweight neural networks for improved extreme weather detection, and machine learning approaches for identifying hydrological extremes under global climate change.

background_concepts.concept: ERA5 reanalysis fields as inputs for event detection
background_concepts.why_needed: Detection models are trained on reanalysis meteorological fields, assuming the reader understands their provenance and resolution.

suggested_resources.title: ERA5 atmospheric reanalysis (UCAR Climate Data Guide)

case_studies.name: Satellite Land Cover and Water Body Segmentation
case_studies.description: This domain focuses on segmentation and classification of land cover, land use, and water bodies from satellite and aerial imagery using deep learning. Readings cover large-scale surface water detection, wetland delineation with imbalanced segmentation, aquaculture pond mapping, irrigation detection and method mapping, and solar plant mapping. It also spans methodological advances including data augmentation, few-shot transfer learning, self-supervised and semi-supervised methods, contextual and geographical priors, and scalable cloud-based LULC classification.

background_concepts.concept: Semantic segmentation for water body detection
background_concepts.why_needed: Surface-water detection datasets assume the reader knows pixel-wise semantic segmentation and evaluation metrics like mean IoU.

suggested_resources.title: Semantic segmentation with U-Net (overview and IoU/Dice metrics)

case_studies.name: Social Media and Crowdsourced Disaster Response Analytics
case_studies.description: This domain examines the use of social media, crowdsourced, and citizen-reported data for understanding and responding to climate disasters. Readings include analyzing Twitter discourse around Cyclone Amphan, improving flood maps through crowdsourcing and semantic segmentation, and recognizing drought impacts from Twitter data and drought impact reports using deep learning and XGBoost.

case_studies.name: Flood & Disaster Risk Mapping and Response Systems
case_studies.description: This domain examines technological systems for assessing, monitoring, and responding to disasters, including satellite-based damage assessment and risk monitoring. It covers specialized infrastructure such as nationwide lightning detection networks for weather forecasting in the Philippines, as well as broader systems architecture approaches to disaster risk reduction.

background_concepts.concept: Systems architecture / model-based systems engineering for disaster response
background_concepts.why_needed: The DRR systems-architecture paper assumes familiarity with formal systems-engineering practice for designing interoperable response systems.

suggested_resources.title: Systems Engineering Fundamentals (Defense Acquisition University guide)

case_studies.name: Rural Water Supply Infrastructure Monitoring & Operations
case_studies.description: This domain focuses on tracking the functionality and usage of rural water infrastructure, exemplified by electronic sensors deployed in Plateau State, Nigeria. It also addresses digital mapping platforms, such as community water system mapping, designed to support the operators who manage rural water systems.

case_studies.name: Drinking Water Quality, Safety & Contamination Interventions
case_studies.description: This domain addresses drinking water safety through interventions targeting contamination, including arsenic-safe water technology and willingness-to-pay studies in West Bengal, India. It also covers technological solutions like air-to-water generators in Kerala and investigations into urban underground water contamination.

background_concepts.why_needed: The arsenic-safe-water study uses WTP survey methods to value a non-market good, assuming familiarity with the stated-preference/contingent valuation approach.

suggested_resources.title: Applications of the contingent valuation method in developing countries (FAO)

case_studies.name: Community-Based & Indigenous Knowledge Climate Resilience
case_studies.description: This domain explores how communities and indigenous knowledge contribute to climate resilience, including profiling disaster-stricken communities and digitizing indigenous knowledge. It emphasizes participatory and co-design approaches, such as empowering Bangladeshi communities on water safety and co-designing microclimate sensors.

case_studies.name: Gender, Empowerment & Marginalization in Water Programs
case_studies.description: This domain examines the intersection of gender empowerment and water-related programs, including vetiver grass technology for river restoration and women's empowerment in rural India. It highlights training rural women to monitor water quality and integrating agriculture with clean energy to enhance livelihoods and WASH empowerment.

background_concepts.why_needed: The women's water-quality monitoring paper frames its impact in terms of WASH empowerment, an institutional/programmatic acronym used without definition.

suggested_resources.title: WASH (Water, Sanitation and Hygiene) — UNICEF

case_studies.name: Satellite-Derived Socio-Economic & Vulnerability Mapping
case_studies.description: This domain uses satellite imagery and remote sensing to map socio-economic indicators, poverty, and vulnerability at high resolution. It covers methods including point-to-region co-learning, temporal prediction, time series analysis, and nighttime light reconstruction, with applications spanning Africa, rural India, and urban disadvantaged populations in the United States.

background_concepts.concept: Nighttime lights as an economic-activity proxy
background_concepts.why_needed: Poverty-mapping readings use nighttime lights and daytime imagery as wealth proxies, assuming readers understand this remote-sensing-to-socioeconomics link.

suggested_resources.title: Daytime and Nighttime Remote Sensing Imagery Data (World Bank IEG)

case_studies.name: Satellite Imagery Analysis for Environmental and Socio-economic Monitoring
case_studies.description: This domain applies deep learning and machine learning to satellite imagery for environmental monitoring, including landslide detection, glacier monitoring, soil carbon estimation, and vegetation greenness forecasting. It also addresses methodological challenges such as domain gaps, interpretability, and data augmentation, with studies spanning Lima, the Hindu Kush Himalaya, India, and Karachi.

background_concepts.concept: Vegetation greenness / spectral vegetation indices (e.g., NDVI)
background_concepts.why_needed: Vegetation-greenness forecasting papers use satellite-derived greenness indices as targets, assuming the reader knows how multispectral reflectance encodes vegetation health.

suggested_resources.title: An Introduction to SAR and Its Applications / Fundamentals of Remote Sensing (NASA ARSET)

case_studies.name: Computational Hydrology and Terrain Analysis Tooling
case_studies.description: This domain focuses on computational tools and algorithms for analyzing terrain and hydrological features, including deep learning for geomorphological mapping and gully erosion susceptibility. It covers topographic position and landform analysis as well as GPU-accelerated hydrology algorithms for flow accumulation, watershed delineation, and runoff simulation.

background_concepts.concept: Topographic Position Index and DEM-based landform classification
background_concepts.why_needed: Terrain-analysis readings assume familiarity with digital elevation models and derived indices (TPI, slope, flow) used to classify landforms and erosion susceptibility.

suggested_resources.title: Specific yield / hydrogeology terrain concepts primer

case_studies.name: Satellite-based Land-Cover Classification & Mapping
case_studies.description: This domain covers deep learning approaches for classifying land cover and land use from multispectral, hyperspectral, and high-resolution satellite and UAV imagery, spanning objects and ecosystems such as aquaculture ponds, mangroves, forests, farmlands, and urban greenspace. It emphasizes methodological challenges including data augmentation, dataset creation for semantic segmentation, self-supervised and multiple-instance learning, and assessing data limitations. Case studies range from region-specific datasets like IndiaSat to greenspace quantification in Karachi and LULC change mapping in Rwanda.

background_concepts.why_needed: These readings classify land cover from satellite imagery whose input channels are spectral bands (red, NIR, etc.); the papers assume readers understand how surfaces reflect different wavelengths but never explain it.

suggested_resources.title: NASA Earthdata: Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI)
GIS Geography: What is NDVI (Normalized Difference Vegetation Index)?

background_concepts.why_needed: The clustering-augmented land cover paper builds on self-supervised representation learning to reduce label needs, but assumes the reader already knows the pretext-task paradigm.

suggested_resources.title: Self-supervised learning for vision foundation models (Zeeshan Khan Suri)

case_studies.name: Remote Sensing for Deforestation & Forest Change Detection
case_studies.description: This domain focuses on detecting and measuring forest and land change over time using multi-temporal satellite and radar imagery, including reviews of Earth observation methods in the tropics and applications like selective logging and reforestation detection. It features deep learning and time-series techniques such as BFAST, echo state networks, cascade frameworks, and the Segment Anything Model for change detection. Applications extend from the Brazilian Amazon and Kenya to fine-resolution vegetation monitoring and detection of destroyed settlements in Nigeria.

background_concepts.concept: BFAST (Breaks For Additive Season and Trend) time-series decomposition
background_concepts.why_needed: The multi-type forest change detection paper builds directly on BFAST applied to Landsat time series but does not explain how the algorithm decomposes a signal into trend/season/remainder to flag breakpoints.

suggested_resources.title: BFAST project overview (r-forge)
SEPAL documentation: Time series analysis with BFAST

background_concepts.why_needed: The selective-logging and human-driven alteration papers fuse radar with optical imagery because SAR penetrates cloud/canopy; the readings assume familiarity with active radar sensing and backscatter.

suggested_resources.title: NASA Earthdata: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR)
NASA ARSET: An Introduction to SAR and Its Applications (Part 1)

case_studies.name: Forest Carbon & Biomass Estimation via Remote Sensing
case_studies.description: This domain covers quantifying forest carbon stocks, fluxes, aboveground biomass, and canopy height using remote sensing tools such as GEDI laser ranging, Landsat, and SAR combined with machine learning. Topics include global carbon flux mapping, uncertainty-aware estimation via mixture density networks, geospatial foundation models, and carbon sequestration assessment including urban forests and tree age discrimination. It also incorporates sensing hardware like the Fluxbot CO2 flux chamber and broader reviews of AI in sustainable forestry.

background_concepts.concept: LiDAR / laser altimetry for canopy height
background_concepts.why_needed: The GEDI/canopy-height and biomass papers depend on spaceborne LiDAR to measure vertical forest structure but assume the reader knows how laser ranging retrieves height and structure.

suggested_resources.title: NSF NEON: The Basics of LiDAR - Light Detection and Ranging
NASA ARSET: The Fundamentals of LiDAR

background_concepts.concept: Aboveground biomass and carbon flux/stock accounting
background_concepts.why_needed: These readings estimate aboveground biomass and convert it to carbon stocks and flux, assuming the reader understands the biomass-to-carbon conversion and sink/source terminology.

suggested_resources.title: NASA Earthdata / SAR Handbook: SAR Methods for Mapping and Monitoring Forest Biomass

case_studies.name: Carbon Offset & REDD+ Integrity
case_studies.description: This domain examines the credibility and accuracy of forest carbon offset and REDD+ programs, highlighting problems such as systematic over-crediting in California's program and overstated emission reductions from voluntary REDD+ projects in the Brazilian Amazon. It also addresses machine learning benchmarks for equitable monitoring, reporting, and verification of nature-based solutions.

background_concepts.why_needed: Both over-crediting papers argue that credits exceed real reductions relative to a baseline; understanding additionality and baseline scenarios is essential to follow their critique.

suggested_resources.title: Carbon Offset Guide: Additionality
Carbon Market Watch: Additionality & Baselines

background_concepts.concept: REDD+ mechanism (MRV, forest reference emission levels)
background_concepts.why_needed: The voluntary REDD+ paper assumes readers know the REDD+ crediting framework, its results-based payment logic, and reference emission levels.

suggested_resources.title: UNFCCC: What is REDD+?

case_studies.name: Community-Based Forest Governance, Tenure & Participatory Mapping
case_studies.description: This domain addresses community management of forests through the lens of land tenure, payment for environmental services, and participatory approaches to mapping and governance. It includes deep learning applications for transparent PES communication, machine learning for coviability transition pathways, and participatory mapping combined with satellite imagery, as illustrated by cases in India and the Chunati Wildlife Sanctuary.

case_studies.name: Nature-Based Solutions & Ecosystem Valuation
case_studies.description: This domain covers the valuation and planning of nature-based solutions and ecosystems, including causal frameworks for large-scale ecosystem valuation such as wetland benefits for flood mitigation. It incorporates landscape ecology metrics in sustainability planning, machine learning methods like adaptive Dice loss for wetland delineation and deep Gaussian processes for mitigation decision support, alongside critical assessment of the value and limits of nature-based solutions.

background_concepts.why_needed: The wetlands causal-valuation and NbS papers assume readers understand the concept of quantifying ecosystem services (provisioning, regulating) as monetary or welfare benefits.

suggested_resources.title: USGS: LandCarbon / ecosystem carbon sequestration assessment overview

case_studies.name: Biodiversity & Conservation Planning Optimization
case_studies.description: This domain focuses on optimization approaches for conservation and biodiversity planning, including derivative-free optimization frameworks for land conservation decisions and optimizing trees for carbon sequestration. It also addresses constraint-based spatial planning to improve graph connectivity in pursuit of the 30-by-30 Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework target.

background_concepts.why_needed: The connectivity optimization paper frames its objective around the '30 by 30' target of the global biodiversity framework, an institutional mechanism it references without introducing.

suggested_resources.title: UNFCCC / IPCC land-use and biodiversity context (REDD+ land-use page)

case_studies.name: Deforestation Detection & Driver Classification from Satellite Imagery
case_studies.description: This domain covers detecting deforestation and classifying its underlying drivers from satellite imagery using deep learning, including vision transformers (ForestViT), rotation-equivariant segmentation, and interpretable and lightweight attribution models. Applications include forecasting deforestation in the Amazon, tracking illegal cattle ranching, classifying deforestation drivers in Indonesia via ForestNet, and analyzing climate risks to soy-maize cropping from Amazon deforestation.

background_concepts.concept: Vision Transformers vs. CNNs for image classification
background_concepts.why_needed: Driver-classification papers (e.g. ForestViT, rotation-equivariant models) assume the reader knows the architectural distinction between convolutional networks and transformer-based image classifiers, and why equivariance/attention matter for overhead imagery.

suggested_resources.title: Beginner's Guide to Semantic Segmentation (covers CNN backbones)

case_studies.name: Forest Carbon Stock Estimation & Carbon Credit Integrity
case_studies.description: This domain focuses on quantifying forest carbon and ensuring the reliability of carbon credits, using approaches like Bayesian state-space models for deforestation baselines and deep learning with aerial imagery to address overestimation of forest carbon. It also covers AI-driven methods for automating carbon sequestration site assessment and joint industry-university efforts to tackle carbon storage challenges. Collectively, the readings aim to improve the accuracy and trustworthiness of forest carbon accounting and credit markets.

background_concepts.why_needed: Deforestation-baseline and carbon-credit papers rely on estimating a counterfactual (what would have happened without protection), often via synthetic control or state-space causal methods, which are used but not taught.

suggested_resources.title: Introduction to Synthetic Control Methods (Tilburg Science Hub)
Synthetic control — Causal Inference Course notes

case_studies.name: Individual Tree & Canopy Structure Measurement
case_studies.description: This domain covers techniques for measuring tree-level and canopy attributes, including species classification from LiDAR, satellite, and 3D tomographic SAR data, DBH estimation from depth maps, and canopy height and cover mapping at scales from Chicago to India. It also encompasses segmenting dead trees, reconstructing tree structure, assessing urban forest health from street view imagery, and geographically localizing natural objects like palms. Together the readings emphasize fine-grained detection, classification, and structural measurement of trees using diverse remote and ground-level sensing.

background_concepts.why_needed: The tomographic-SAR tree-species paper relies on 3D SAR to resolve vertical canopy structure; readers need baseline SAR sensing knowledge the paper does not provide.

suggested_resources.title: NASA Earthdata: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR)

case_studies.name: Foundation Models & Data-Efficient Learning for Earth Observation
case_studies.description: This domain explores large-scale pretrained and multi-modal foundation models for remote sensing, including forest monitoring models (FoMo), climatic and geographic location encoders, and masked autoencoders for data reconstruction. It also addresses data-efficient strategies such as visual prompt-tuning, few-shot instance segmentation, weakly-semi-supervised detection, data programming for labeling, and fine-tuning language models for biodiversity entity recognition. The readings collectively aim to reduce labeled-data requirements and build general-purpose models for ecological and Earth observation tasks.

background_concepts.concept: Foundation models, self-supervision, and masked autoencoders
background_concepts.why_needed: These papers build geospatial foundation models and masked-autoencoder pretraining, assuming the reader knows the pretraining/fine-tuning paradigm and pretext tasks like masked reconstruction.

suggested_resources.title: Introduction to Earth Foundation Models (IGARSS 2025 tutorial)
Self-supervised learning for vision foundation models (Zeeshan Khan Suri)

case_studies.name: Vegetation & Ecosystem Dynamics Forecasting
case_studies.description: This domain concerns modeling and forecasting vegetation and ecosystem behavior over time, including forest greenness monitoring, phenology prediction, net primary productivity under warming, and drought resilience analysis via Shapley values. It spans data-driven and mechanistic approaches, hybrid and physics-encoded spatiotemporal deep learning, and datasets/challenges like EarthNet2021 for forecasting localized climate impacts. The readings emphasize predicting how vegetation and land surfaces respond to climatic drivers across regions such as Africa and India.

background_concepts.why_needed: Forecasting greenness and phenology relies on time series of NDVI and related indices as the target variable; the papers assume knowledge of what greenness metrics measure.

suggested_resources.title: NASA Earthdata: Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI)

case_studies.name: Community Co-Design & Indigenous Knowledge in Climate Monitoring
case_studies.description: This domain centers on incorporating community perspectives and Indigenous knowledge into climate monitoring and resilience efforts. The reading focuses on capturing and digitizing Indigenous knowledge to strengthen community resilience to climate change, highlighting participatory and co-design approaches to climate data.

case_studies.name: Interpretability & Verification for Climate Finance and Conservation
case_studies.description: This domain addresses making machine learning for climate finance and forest conservation transparent, trustworthy, and verifiable, as in GainForest's interpretable models on satellite imagery and Xingu's explanations of geospatial predictions under weak supervision. It also covers verification methods like TrueBranch's metric-learning-based checks on conservation projects and systems designed to be past- and future-aware when evaluating climate policies. Together the readings emphasize interpretability, accountability, and validation to support credible climate finance decisions.

background_concepts.concept: Metric learning and weak supervision for verification
background_concepts.why_needed: Verification systems (TrueBranch, Xingu, GainForest) rely on metric-learning embeddings and weakly-supervised labels to audit conservation claims, assuming the reader knows these representation-learning and label-efficient techniques.

suggested_resources.title: A Primer on Self-supervised Learning (covers representation/metric learning)

case_studies.name: Species Distribution Modeling from Remote Sensing & Citizen Science
case_studies.description: This domain covers computational methods for predicting where species occur by combining remote sensing imagery, citizen science observations, and machine learning, including foundational tools like MaxEnt and reviews for ML practitioners. Readings span deep learning approaches using Sentinel-2 time-series (e.g., for orchids and birds in SatBird), heterogeneous graph neural networks, and specialized techniques such as imbalance-aware presence-only loss functions and predicting occurrence from partial observations. Together they address scalability, biodiversity monitoring in the Anthropocene, and the practical challenges of modeling species distributions globally.

background_concepts.concept: MaxEnt (maximum entropy) modeling and background/pseudo-absence points
background_concepts.why_needed: The practical MaxEnt guide and the SDM reviews assume the reader knows how MaxEnt fits a presence-only distribution using background points, and why pseudo-absences differ from true absences — but they don't teach this machinery.

suggested_resources.title: A Brief Tutorial on Maxent (Phillips et al., AMNH)
Maxent - Biodiversity Informatics (AMNH overview)

background_concepts.why_needed: The presence-only loss and partial-observation papers assume familiarity with why occurrence records lack true absences, and how spatial/taxonomic sampling bias distorts models — a core statistical premise never explained in the readings themselves.

suggested_resources.title: Best Practices for Using eBird Data — Introduction (Cornell Lab)

background_concepts.why_needed: SatBird and the anthropocene-SDM review use eBird as their observation source, assuming the reader understands the checklist protocol and the strengths/limits of volunteer-collected data.

suggested_resources.title: eBird — European Citizen Science Platform overview

background_concepts.why_needed: The heterogeneous GNN SDM paper assumes the reader knows how message-passing over nodes/edges works; the concept is used as a building block without any introduction.

suggested_resources.title: A Gentle Introduction to Graph Neural Networks (Distill)

case_studies.name: Biodiversity Data Quality & Labeling for Conservation AI
case_studies.description: This domain examines the reliability and cleanliness of datasets used to train conservation AI, exposing how benchmarks like ImageNet misrepresent biodiversity and how citizen-science images contain bugs and errors. Readings explore domain-specific distribution shifts in volunteer-collected data (DivShift), classification under ground-truth uncertainty in camera traps, and efficient labeling via data programming on geospatial information (GeoLabels). The focus is on building robust models by improving data curation and handling label noise.

background_concepts.why_needed: DivShift and the camera-trap paper are framed entirely around 'distribution shifts' and ground-truth uncertainty between train and deployment data, assuming the reader already knows what covariate/label shift means and why it degrades models.

suggested_resources.title: An introduction to domain adaptation and transfer learning (Kouw & Loog)
Tackling Domain Shift in AI: A Deep Dive into Domain Adaptation

background_concepts.concept: Data programming / weak supervision for labels
background_concepts.why_needed: GeoLabels builds on 'data programming' to generate labels from noisy heuristics, a labeling paradigm the paper names but does not explain.

suggested_resources.title: A Gentle Introduction to Graph Neural Networks (Distill) — general ML background

case_studies.name: Automated Wildlife Monitoring via Sensors & Bioacoustics
case_studies.description: This domain focuses on continuous, automated tracking of wildlife using acoustic sensors and on-edge computing, including agile modeling workflows for bioacoustic monitoring. Readings address running energy-efficient deep learning models on edge devices for critter monitoring and long-term observation of bird flocks in the wild. The emphasis is on practical, scalable sensor-based approaches to biodiversity surveillance.

background_concepts.why_needed: The bioacoustic monitoring papers assume the reader knows sounds are converted to time-frequency spectrograms and processed as species classification / call segmentation / sound-event-detection tasks, without teaching this representation.

suggested_resources.title: Introduction to bioacoustics (CRAN vignette)

case_studies.name: Forest & Vegetation Monitoring from Satellite/UAV Imagery
case_studies.description: This domain covers remote sensing techniques for mapping and characterizing forests and vegetation, using satellite, UAV, and LiDAR data to estimate tree diversity, classify species, and detect canopy changes. Readings include spectral variability approaches for tropical forests, multi-temporal NDVI analysis of California floristic richness, multimodal and few-shot segmentation methods, and fine-tuning foundation models like EarthPT for tree canopy mapping. Applications range from detecting human-driven forest alterations to fine-resolution vegetation monitoring from space.

background_concepts.why_needed: These papers estimate canopy, tree diversity, and floristic richness from multispectral reflectance and assume the reader understands red/near-infrared bands and NDVI-type indices as measures of vegetation greenness.

suggested_resources.title: Measuring Vegetation (NDVI & EVI) — NASA Earth Observatory
NDVI, the Foundation for Remote Sensing Phenology — USGS

background_concepts.why_needed: The U-Net, forest-terrain, and segment-then-classify papers assume the reader knows pixel-wise semantic segmentation as a task and its encoder-decoder architectures.

suggested_resources.title: A Gentle Introduction to Graph Neural Networks (Distill) — deep-learning building blocks

case_studies.name: Anti-Poaching & Illegal-Activity Detection
case_studies.description: This domain applies AI and remote sensing to detect and predict illegal activities threatening wildlife, such as poaching and illegal cattle ranching visible in high-resolution satellite imagery. Readings include generative AI methods for poaching prediction (latent composite flow matching), weakly-semi-supervised object detection, and active learning with multimodal imagery to locate rhino habitats. The focus is on proactive conservation enforcement through detection and prediction.

background_concepts.concept: Green security games and patrol resource allocation
background_concepts.why_needed: Poaching-prediction work in this area is grounded in the green-security-game / Stackelberg framing (as in PAWS) for allocating scarce enforcement resources, a mechanism referenced but not explained.

suggested_resources.title: PAWS: game-theoretic patrolling application (AI Magazine)

background_concepts.why_needed: The rhino-horn paper hinges on active learning to prioritize which multimodal images to label under scarce budgets, assuming the reader knows the query/label loop.

suggested_resources.title: An introduction to domain adaptation and transfer learning (Kouw & Loog) — related labeled-data-scarcity framing

case_studies.name: Phenology & Ecological Response to Climate
case_studies.description: This domain studies the timing of biological events like flowering and dormancy and how species respond to climate, using drone imagery, satellite sensing, and deep learning. Readings cover cost-effective flowering monitoring of tropical trees, hybrid models for temperature effects on tree dormancy, buffelgrass green-up prediction, insect range shifts via out-of-distribution detection, and wildflower phenology from expert-annotated images. The emphasis is on tracking and forecasting ecological responses to a changing climate.

background_concepts.concept: Growing degree days, chill units, and tree dormancy
background_concepts.why_needed: The hybrid dormancy-prediction paper builds ML on top of classical chilling/forcing accumulation models (GDD, chill requirements, endo/ecodormancy) that it treats as known mechanistic background.

suggested_resources.title: Growing degree-day (Wikipedia primer)
Calculating Degree Days — Wisconsin Horticulture Extension

background_concepts.why_needed: The insect range-shift paper frames shifting ranges as an OOD-detection problem, assuming the reader knows what OOD detection and distribution shift mean.

suggested_resources.title: An introduction to domain adaptation and transfer learning (Kouw & Loog)

case_studies.name: Wildfire & Climate Hazard Prediction from Remote Sensing
case_studies.description: This domain leverages remote sensing and deep learning to map, detect, and predict wildfires and related climate hazards, including burned area mapping and short-term fire hazard forecasting. Readings address semantic segmentation of active fire on unbalanced classes, semi-supervised domain adaptation for wildfire detection, prototype-oriented change detection for disaster management, and predicting concurrent heatwaves, droughts, and wildfires. The focus is on hazard prediction and disaster response, with some related SAR-based detection methods.

background_concepts.why_needed: The SAR ship-detection and several hazard-mapping papers assume the reader understands active radar backscatter imaging and its all-weather advantages over optical sensors.

suggested_resources.title: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) — NASA Earthdata basics
An Introduction to SAR and Its Applications, Part 1 — NASA ARSET

background_concepts.why_needed: The wildfire detection paper relies on adapting a model across geographic/sensor domains with limited target labels, a technique it names but does not introduce.

suggested_resources.title: An introduction to domain adaptation and transfer learning (Kouw & Loog)

case_studies.name: Community-Engaged & Indigenous-Led Conservation
case_studies.description: This domain highlights conservation efforts that center local communities, Indigenous knowledge, and human-in-the-loop collaboration, including media monitoring for low-resource languages and managing wild salmon fisheries in Indigenous rivers. Readings blend technical multimodal AI approaches with narrative conservation stories such as saving jungle habitats and rescuing 'dancing' bears. The emphasis is on culturally grounded, participatory approaches to environmental stewardship.

background_concepts.why_needed: The wild-salmon expert-in-the-loop paper co-manages rivers with Indigenous communities, assuming familiarity with the governance norms (CARE, data sovereignty) that shape how such data can ethically be used.

suggested_resources.title: CARE Principles for Indigenous Data Governance — ARDC

case_studies.name: Aquatic & Freshwater Ecosystem Conservation
case_studies.description: This domain addresses the monitoring and protection of aquatic and freshwater ecosystems, from predicting seagrass ecosystem states with graph neural networks to building datasets for aquatic invertebrates in Vietnam. Readings also include global optimization methods for conserving freshwater fishes under the UN's 30x30 goal. The focus spans marine and freshwater biodiversity, spanning data collection, ecosystem state prediction, and conservation planning.

background_concepts.why_needed: The freshwater-fish paper optimizes connected components for protected-area planning under the global '30x30' conservation target, an institutional goal it references without explanation.

suggested_resources.title: A Gentle Introduction to Graph Neural Networks (Distill) — connectivity/graph background

background_concepts.why_needed: The seagrass ecosystem-state paper predicts using GNNs over spatial graphs, assuming knowledge of message passing on graph-structured data.

suggested_resources.title: A Gentle Introduction to Graph Neural Networks (Distill)

case_studies.name: Conservation Foundation Models & Transferable Representations
case_studies.description: This domain develops general-purpose, pretrained models and embeddings that transfer across ecological and geospatial tasks, including climatic implicit embeddings (Climplicit) and pretrained geographic location encoders. Readings cover masked autoencoders for reconstructing remote sensing data (CuMoLoS-MAE) and fine-tuning language models for biodiversity named entity recognition to curate environmental knowledge graphs. The emphasis is on reusable, transferable representations that support diverse conservation applications.

background_concepts.why_needed: CuMoLoS-MAE and the pretrained-encoder papers assume the reader knows the mask-then-reconstruct self-supervised paradigm used to learn transferable representations without labels.

suggested_resources.title: Masked Auto-Encoders as Scalable Vision Learners (ECCV 2022 tutorial slides)

background_concepts.why_needed: Climplicit and the geographic-location-encoder paper hinge on the idea of learned vector embeddings of location/climate that transfer across tasks, a representation-learning concept used but not taught.

suggested_resources.title: A Gentle Introduction to Graph Neural Networks (Distill) — representation/embedding background

case_studies.name: AI-based human-wildlife conflict monitoring
case_studies.description: This domain focuses on using AI and media analysis to detect and understand human-wildlife conflicts, including few-shot approaches for environmental conservation media monitoring in low-resource languages. It covers combining news articles and aerial imagery to identify conflict hotspots, as demonstrated in the Indian context, and extends to interpreting public perception of weather disasters through metaphor analysis. Together, these readings emphasize leveraging textual and visual data sources to monitor and interpret human-environment tensions.

background_concepts.concept: Few-shot / low-resource NLP for media mining
background_concepts.why_needed: The conservation media-monitoring paper detects conflict signals from news in low-resource languages, assuming the reader knows few-shot learning and the low-resource-language problem in NLP.

suggested_resources.title: An introduction to domain adaptation and transfer learning (Kouw & Loog)

case_studies.name: Digitizing indigenous ecological knowledge for climate resilience
case_studies.description: This domain addresses the capture and digitization of indigenous knowledge to strengthen community resilience against climate change. It explores multimodal foundation AI paired with expert-in-the-loop methods for sustainably managing wild salmon fisheries in indigenous rivers, as well as efforts to rescue and preserve endangered cultural and ecological practices. The readings collectively highlight how technology can support and safeguard traditional ecological knowledge for climate adaptation.

background_concepts.concept: Indigenous data sovereignty and CARE vs FAIR governance
background_concepts.why_needed: Efforts to digitize community-held knowledge raise data-ownership and marginalization questions that presume familiarity with Indigenous data governance frameworks (CARE, and how they complement FAIR) — the crux of ethical design here.

suggested_resources.title: The CARE Principles for Indigenous Data Governance (Data Science Journal)
CARE Principles for Indigenous Data Governance — ARDC guide

case_studies.name: Voice-based community media platforms in rural India
case_studies.description: This domain examines mobile and IVR-based community media systems like Mobile Vaani and CGNet Swara that enable citizen journalism, social development, and interaction among low-income rural populations in India. Readings cover design lessons, community mobilization strategies, impact pathways, and technical innovations such as automated voice-based question-answering and voice versus keypress surveys. It also addresses inclusive engagement of remote and marginalized communities, including COVID-19 response networks and WhatsApp-based journalism tools.

background_concepts.concept: Interactive Voice Response (IVR) and DTMF signaling
background_concepts.why_needed: Nearly every reading here describes voice forums built on IVR, keypad (DTMF) navigation, and text-to-speech, but assumes the reader already understands how an automated telephony menu system captures and routes caller input.

suggested_resources.title: What Is IVR? - Interactive Voice Response Explained (AWS)
What Is Interactive Voice Response (IVR)? (IBM Think)

case_studies.name: Community radio operations and automation
case_studies.description: This domain focuses on the technical and operational infrastructure supporting community radio stations in India, including automation systems, content distribution architectures for rural areas, and answering machine systems like Phonepeti. Readings explore how ICTs meet the operational needs of these stations, as well as their commercialization and interactive programming such as singing competitions combining radio with IVRS.

background_concepts.concept: Community radio licensing and regulation in India (GOPA, CRS policy)
background_concepts.why_needed: These readings discuss running community radio stations in India within a specific institutional and legal framework (non-profit eligibility, content quotas, advertising limits, the Grant of Permission Agreement) that they reference without explaining.

suggested_resources.title: Policy Guidelines for Setting up Community Radio Stations in India (MIB / Broadcast Seva)
How to Start a Community Radio Station (CRS): Step by Step Guide

case_studies.name: Computational detection and analysis of media bias and political polarization
case_studies.description: This domain covers quantitative and computational approaches to measuring media bias, political polarization, and information diffusion across blogs, Twitter, and news coverage, in both US and Indian contexts. Readings range from crowdsourced and aspect-level bias analysis to studies of polarization, structural virality, and the political economy of mass media. It also engages with the Hindi public sphere and politicians' use of social media platforms.

background_concepts.why_needed: Blogosphere and Twitter polarization studies quantify partisan clustering using graph-theoretic measures like modularity and assortative mixing, which are used as the analytic backbone but not derived or explained in the papers.

suggested_resources.title: Network Analysis and Modeling, CSCI 5352 Lecture 5 (Aaron Clauset, assortativity & modularity)
Social Network Analysis: Homophily (Donglei Du lecture notes)

case_studies.name: Community networks and grassroots connectivity infrastructure
case_studies.description: This domain addresses grassroots and community-owned connectivity solutions, including networks powered by community currencies and assisted digital distribution platforms for underserved populations. Readings emphasize participatory research with global community networks and the sustainability role played by women in rural networks. It also considers connectivity perspectives among small non-profit organizations serving marginalized groups like street children in Bangladesh.

background_concepts.why_needed: The community-currency-powered network reading proposes local currencies as a sustainability model without explaining what a complementary/community currency is or how mutual-credit and local exchange systems function.

suggested_resources.title: Complementary currency (Wikipedia overview)
What complementary currencies look like (Mediterranean Co-Production Lab lesson)

background_concepts.concept: Wireless mesh networks and last-mile backhaul
background_concepts.why_needed: Grassroots connectivity readings assume familiarity with how community networks are physically built—mesh nodes, gateways, backhaul, and multi-hop routing—which underpins the sustainability and maintenance discussions but is not taught.

suggested_resources.title: Over The Air: Community Wireless and Mesh Networking Primer (Tech Learning Collective)
How Wireless Mesh Networks Work (HowStuffWorks)

case_studies.name: WhatsApp and social media adoption in the Global South
case_studies.description: This domain explores how platforms like WhatsApp and Facebook are adopted and used in urban and rural India, and the broader Global South, examining sentiment, empowerment, and the social meaning of ICTs in context. Readings also probe the darker dynamics of social media, including fake news in developing countries and the filter bubble effects that shape information exposure. Together they map the mobile media actor-network and patterns of technology usage.

background_concepts.why_needed: The mobile media actor-network reading frames adoption through actor-network theory, treating human and non-human actors symmetrically, but assumes the reader knows this sociological framework rather than introducing it.

suggested_resources.title: The social meaning of ICTs / media theory context — Journalism University explainer on the public sphere and networked media

case_studies.name: Gendered access to mobile phones and voice interfaces
case_studies.description: This domain investigates how gender shapes access to and use of mobile phones and voice interfaces, including experimental work on whether the gender and accent of an IVR voice matter. Readings examine women livestock keepers using mobile phones in Zimbabwe and how personal characteristics and intermediaries influence girls' mobile appropriation in Nairobi. It highlights gendered barriers and mediation in mobile and mobile internet access.

case_studies.name: Women-led participatory media initiatives
case_studies.description: This domain centers on media platforms designed and run by women, drawing on experiences from a participatory media platform for and led by women in rural North India. It also features Khabar Lahariya, a rural women-run news network, illustrating how women lead grassroots journalism and content production. Together the readings foreground women's agency in participatory media.

case_studies.name: AI-assisted content moderation and monitoring for climate/environmental discourse
case_studies.description: This domain examines AI-based approaches to content moderation and media monitoring, particularly for environmental and climate discourse, including few-shot conservation media monitoring in low-resource languages and two-stage frameworks for climate content categorization. Readings also analyze moderation complexities in voice-based participatory media, AI risk coverage in news media, and community-centric design of text-to-image AI tools. It bridges technical moderation methods with their social and environmental implications.

background_concepts.why_needed: The environmental conservation media monitoring work centers on few-shot learning to classify content in low-resource languages, assuming the reader understands N-way-K-shot setups and why limited labeled data motivates this approach.

suggested_resources.title: Few-Shot Learning & Meta-Learning Tutorial (RBC Borealis)
Few Shot Learning (GeeksforGeeks, support/query sets)

case_studies.name: Media and information access for displaced and disabled populations
case_studies.description: This domain addresses how marginalized populations, including visually impaired users, access media and information technologies, such as through case studies on IVR system use. Readings also examine Rohingya refugees' quest for democratic participation in the digital age and the limits of design in ensuring responsible technological outcomes for vulnerable groups. It emphasizes equity and responsible design for displaced and disabled communities.

case_studies.name: Mobile phones and market efficiency in developing economies
case_studies.description: This domain examines how mobile phones affect economic development and market functioning in developing regions, drawing on the well-known case of the fishing industry in Kerala, India, and its subsequent critique emphasizing the importance of complex social systems. It also covers the domestication of mobile payments by underserved South African microentrepreneurs and broader reflections on the unpredictable social and economic impacts of mobile technology.

background_concepts.concept: Law of one price / market price dispersion
background_concepts.why_needed: The fishing-market studies argue mobile phones reduce price dispersion across markets, invoking the economic 'law of one price' as the benchmark for market efficiency without explaining it.

suggested_resources.title: The Law of One Price (Corporate Finance Institute)

case_studies.name: Community and small-scale cellular/network infrastructure
case_studies.description: This domain focuses on locally owned and operated telecommunications infrastructure, including small-scale, sustainable cellular networks and community networks financed through community currencies. It also addresses technical challenges such as measurement scheduling strategies in low-resource network environments.

background_concepts.concept: GSM cellular network architecture (base station, spectrum, backhaul)
background_concepts.why_needed: The community cellular papers assume familiarity with how a GSM cell site works (base transceiver station, spectrum licensing, backhaul to the core network) when describing small-scale locally-run networks.

suggested_resources.title: How Cell Phones Work (U.S. FCC consumer guide)
Cellular Concepts and GSM Basics (NPTEL lecture notes)

case_studies.name: Rural electrification via microgrids and minigrids
case_studies.description: This domain covers the engineering, planning, and evaluation of microgrid and minigrid systems for rural electrification, spanning cost estimation of micro-hydropower in Nepal, rapid feasibility and business model assessments, DC microgrid power-electronic loss modeling, and multicriteria decision analysis for projects in Sub-Saharan Africa. It also incorporates social perspectives, applying social practice theory to user demand and case studies of standalone, minigrid, and grid-connected system design with irrigation loads in Ethiopia.

background_concepts.why_needed: These engineering papers design standalone minigrids and DC microgrids assuming the reader knows what a microgrid is, its components (generation, storage, loads), and the concept of islanded vs. grid-connected operation.

suggested_resources.title: Microgrids (DOE Tribal Energy Guide)
Microgrid Conceptual Design Guidebook (Sandia National Laboratories)

background_concepts.why_needed: The electrification-project selection paper is built entirely around choosing an MCDA method (weights, criteria, ranking of alternatives) but does not teach what MCDA is or how weighting/scoring works.

suggested_resources.title: An Introductory Guide to Multi-Criteria Decision Analysis (UK Government Analysis Function)
A Practical Guide to Multi-Criteria Decision Analysis (Victoria University of Wellington)

case_studies.name: Electricity provision and the political economy of the grid
case_studies.description: This domain explores the political and institutional dimensions of electricity access, including how electoral cycles influence electricity provision and losses in India. It also addresses tools and education for energy access, such as blackout mapping via smartphones (GridWatch), workshops for electricity access educators, and critical debate over whether technology alone can deliver universal energy access.

background_concepts.why_needed: The election-cycle studies claim causal effects of political incentives on electricity supply using quasi-experimental designs (special elections, difference-in-differences) without explaining the identification strategy or parallel-trends assumption.

suggested_resources.title: Difference-in-Differences (World Bank DIME Wiki)
Difference-in-Differences (Causal Inference: The Mixtape)

background_concepts.concept: Distribution losses / aggregate technical & commercial (AT&C) losses
background_concepts.why_needed: The electricity-loss paper measures 'line losses' as a manipulable political variable, assuming the reader understands technical vs. non-technical (theft/billing) distribution losses in a power grid.

suggested_resources.title: Electricity explained: How electricity is delivered (U.S. EIA)

case_studies.name: Satellite/remote-sensing mapping of infrastructure and socioeconomic conditions
case_studies.description: This domain applies satellite and remote-sensing imagery with deep learning to map infrastructure and socioeconomic conditions, including poverty mapping, urban village identification, road and infrastructure network extraction, and truck and commercial vehicle traffic detection. It also encompasses nighttime light reconstruction, rare energy infrastructure detection, vulnerability mapping of urban disadvantaged populations, and tracking rural development over decades.

background_concepts.concept: Semantic segmentation and object detection in imagery
background_concepts.why_needed: These papers infer roads, buildings, and infrastructure from imagery using segmentation and detection architectures, assuming the reader knows pixel-level labeling, bounding boxes, and CNN-based vision pipelines.

suggested_resources.title: Beginner's Guide to Semantic Segmentation (V7 Labs)
Semantic Segmentation (MathWorks)

background_concepts.concept: Nighttime lights as a socioeconomic proxy
background_concepts.why_needed: The nighttime-light reconstruction and poverty-mapping papers treat satellite-measured night light as a proxy for economic activity and electrification without explaining the DMSP/VIIRS data or the light-GDP correlation.

suggested_resources.title: Nighttime Lights (NASA Earthdata)

case_studies.name: AI for power systems modeling and grid resilience
case_studies.description: This domain uses machine learning and optimization to model, simulate, and strengthen power systems, covering optimal power flow learning, operator learning, power flow benchmark datasets, and open-source microgrid simulation for AI research. It further addresses grid resilience through reinforcement learning and graph-based methods for mitigating cascading failures and fault chains, smart meter data analytics, and integrated resilience platforms like GRIP.

background_concepts.concept: Optimal power flow (OPF) and power flow equations
background_concepts.why_needed: Several papers learn or approximate OPF solutions and use power-flow benchmarks, assuming the reader knows AC/DC power flow equations, generator dispatch, and line-flow constraints.

suggested_resources.title: Introduction to Optimal Power Flow (TU Delft OpenCourseWare)
Lecture Notes on Optimal Power Flow (DTU, Chatzivasileiadis)

background_concepts.why_needed: The fault-chain search and cascading-failure mitigation papers assume understanding of how a single line/component overload propagates into a system-wide blackout.

suggested_resources.title: Tutorial: Cascading Failures (Graph-TIGER documentation)
Grid Systems / Microgrids overview (U.S. DOE Office of Electricity)

case_studies.name: Solar energy forecasting and rooftop/asset assessment
case_studies.description: This domain focuses on assessing solar resources and assets, including rooftop solar potential estimation, solar panel mapping via oriented object detection, and experimental study of panel soiling effects in Ghana. It also covers near-term and ultra-short-term photovoltaic power forecasting using satellite and cloud-coverage data, alongside community-oriented solar deployment emphasizing maintenance and empowerment in Haiti.

background_concepts.concept: Photovoltaic energy conversion and system components
background_concepts.why_needed: The solar forecasting and rooftop-potential papers assume familiarity with how PV cells/modules generate power, tilt/orientation, and factors (irradiance, shading, cloud cover) that drive output.

suggested_resources.title: How Does Solar Work? (U.S. DOE)
Photovoltaics and electricity (U.S. EIA Energy Explained)

background_concepts.concept: Oriented object detection in remote sensing
background_concepts.why_needed: The solar-panel mapping paper uses 'oriented' (rotated) bounding boxes rather than standard axis-aligned detection, a specialized variant the reader must understand.

suggested_resources.title: Beginner's Guide to Semantic Segmentation / object detection (V7 Labs)

case_studies.name: Sensing and data-driven optimization of local energy demand and services
case_studies.description: This domain leverages sensing and data-driven methods to characterize and optimize local energy demand and services, including granular hourly energy forecasting from monthly invoices, modeling residential and lighting demand in Kenya and East Africa, and causal analysis of cooking fuel access and emissions. It also addresses off-grid system data sharing in Malawi, battery repurposing for solar storage in Pacific Island communities, latrine sensors for sanitation, and community energy usage and storage analysis.

background_concepts.concept: Electricity load profiles and demand modeling
background_concepts.why_needed: These papers estimate hourly load profiles and induced residential demand, assuming the reader understands load curves, peak/base load, and load factor as characterizations of electricity demand.

suggested_resources.title: Electricity explained: Use of electricity / demand (U.S. EIA)

case_studies.name: Disaster and climate risk detection for infrastructure resilience
case_studies.description: This domain applies AI and systems approaches to detect and manage disaster and climate risks to infrastructure, including flood-related waste mapping, building damage assessment from satellite imagery, transportation resilience to floods, and landslide and illegal mining detection. It also covers housing stock characterization from drone images for climate resilience, systems architectures for disaster risk reduction, telecommunications-assisted remote monitoring, and shade simulation for heat resilience.

background_concepts.why_needed: The damage-assessment papers classify pre/post-disaster building damage from satellite imagery, assuming knowledge of remote-sensing classification pipelines and change detection.

suggested_resources.title: Beginner's Guide to Semantic Segmentation (V7 Labs)

case_studies.name: Infrastructure and design in informal settlements
case_studies.description: This domain examines the physical infrastructure and construction practices of informal settlements, including whether high-mast lighting adequately serves pedestrian paths. It also explores the construction process in its spatial, temporal, and cultural dimensions through the case of Mathare, Nairobi.

case_studies.name: Rural telecenters and shared-access digital service delivery
case_studies.description: This domain examines shared-access computing models that bring digital services to underserved rural populations, drawing on systems like KioskNet and assisted distribution platforms such as BlendNet. It also engages with the practical realities of these deployments through case studies of telecenter sustainability failures in the SARI project in India and telecenters fostering inclusion in Bangladesh. Together, the readings weigh both the technical designs and the socioeconomic challenges of making shared digital access viable and inclusive.

background_concepts.why_needed: KioskNet is built on store-and-forward, delay-tolerant networking to serve intermittently-connected rural kiosks, assuming the reader understands DTN and the bundle/mechanical-backhaul model.

suggested_resources.title: Delay- and Disruption-Tolerant Networks: A Tutorial (NASA)

case_studies.name: Telecom infrastructure and macroeconomic development
case_studies.description: This domain explores the relationship between telecommunications infrastructure and broader economic growth in developing nations. It focuses on how investment in and access to telecom services contribute to national and regional development outcomes.

background_concepts.why_needed: The macro study estimates telecom penetration's effect on GDP growth using cross-country growth regressions and invokes network externalities/critical mass, terms it does not define.

suggested_resources.title: Network Effects / Externalities (Corporate Finance Institute)

case_studies.name: Off-grid and mini-grid rural electrification
case_studies.description: This domain addresses decentralized electrification efforts aimed at bringing reliable power to rural communities lacking grid access. Grounded in a rural microgrid field pilot in India, it considers both the technical goal of ensuring dependable electricity supply and the accompanying social upliftment benefits.

background_concepts.concept: Off-grid solar systems: storage sizing and business models
background_concepts.why_needed: The field-pilot paper discusses battery storage, panel sizing, and pay-as-you-go/tariff business models for off-grid microgrids, assuming the reader understands these energy-access fundamentals.

suggested_resources.title: Microgrid Overview (U.S. DOE Grid Deployment Office fact sheet)
Solar Photovoltaic System Design Basics (U.S. DOE)

case_studies.name: Power grid reliability monitoring and analytics
case_studies.description: This domain applies machine learning and natural language processing to understand and improve energy systems, including identifying indicators of energy burden and analyzing power grid outages in Uganda. The readings emphasize data-driven, knowledge-informed policy approaches to grid reliability. A central theme is leveraging analytics to promote an equitable transition of energy systems.

background_concepts.concept: Energy burden and energy equity metrics
background_concepts.why_needed: The energy-burden and equitable-transition papers center on 'energy burden' (share of household income spent on energy) as a policy metric without defining it.

suggested_resources.title: Low-Income Energy Affordability Data (LEAD) Tool & energy burden (U.S. DOE)

case_studies.name: Rural and last-mile network connectivity measurement
case_studies.description: This domain focuses on empirically measuring the state of cellular and data connectivity in rural and last-mile areas, particularly through 2G/3G network measurements and assessments of cellular data in India. It connects these measurement efforts to practical applications such as improving emergency preparedness and response in rural areas. The readings collectively characterize connectivity gaps and their real-world implications.

background_concepts.concept: Cellular signal metrics (RSSI, Ec/Io, RSCP, throughput) for 2G/3G
background_concepts.why_needed: These measurement studies report signal strength and quality using metrics like RSSI and Ec/Io and cellular-generation terminology without defining them.

suggested_resources.title: Understanding RSSI, RSRP, and RSRQ (5Gstore)
Beyond Signal Bars: Understanding RSSI, RSRP, SINR & Other Key Metrics (Teltonika)

case_studies.name: Digital inclusion for marginalized users and small organizations
case_studies.description: This domain investigates how marginalized users, small non-profits, and underserved communities access and benefit from digital technologies, spanning citizen engagement apps in Windhoek and equitable community internet access during the COVID-19 pandemic. It also addresses securing information systems in an unequal world and understanding the connectivity perspectives of small non-profits serving street children in Bangladesh. The readings emphasize bridging the digital divide across diverse and often overlooked populations.

case_studies.name: Women in Computing and Gendered Barriers to Technology Access
case_studies.description: Examines the structural, cultural, and policy barriers shaping women's access to and participation in computing and mobile technology in the Global South, including stigma, discourse framing, and participatory approaches to designing for women's safety and empowerment.
